configHelpTranslations.Metadata fields automatically maintained: Metadatafält som OpenClaw underhåller automatiskt för att spara skriv- och versionshistorik för den här konfigurationsfilen. Låt systemet hantera värdena och undvik manuella ändringar om du inte felsöker migreringshistorik.
configHelpTranslations.Auto-set when OpenClaw writes the config: Sätts automatiskt när OpenClaw skriver konfigurationen.
configHelpTranslations.Environment import and override settings: Inställningar för miljöimport och åsidosättanden som används för att ge gateway-processen runtime-variabler. Använd avsnittet för att styra inläsning av shell-miljö och explicit variabelinjektion.
configHelpTranslations.Shell environment import controls for lo: Kontroller för att läsa in variabler från ditt inloggningsshell vid uppstart. Låt detta vara aktiverat när du är beroende av profildefinierade hemligheter eller PATH-anpassningar.
configHelpTranslations.Enables loading environment variables fr: Aktiverar inläsning av miljövariabler från användarens shellprofil under uppstart. Behåll aktivt på utvecklarmaskiner, eller stäng av i låsta tjänstemiljöer med explicit miljöhantering.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for: Maximal tid i millisekunder som tillåts för att läsa in shell-miljön innan fallback används. Använd kortare tidsgränser för snabbare uppstart eller högre värden när shell-init är tung.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit key/value environment variable: Explicita nyckel/värde-åsidosättanden för miljövariabler som slås ihop in i OpenClaws runtime-miljö. Använd detta för deterministisk miljökonfiguration i stället för att bara förlita dig på sidoeffekter från shellprofilen.
configHelpTranslations.Setup wizard state tracking fields that: Fält för statusspårning av installationsguiden som sparar detaljer om den senaste guidade onboardingen. Behåll dessa fält för observabilitet och felsökning av setupflöden mellan uppgraderingar.
configHelpTranslations.Diagnostics controls for targeted tracin: Diagnostikinställningar för riktad spårning, telemetryxport och cacheinspektion vid felsökning. Håll grundnivån låg i produktion och aktivera djupare signaler bara när du undersöker problem.
configHelpTranslations.Logging behavior controls for severity,: Kontroller för loggbeteende, nivåer, utmål, formatering och maskering av känslig data. Håll nivåer och maskering tillräckligt strikta för produktion men bevara nyttig diagnostik.
configHelpTranslations.Primary log level threshold for runtime: Primär loggnivå för runtime-loggning: "silent", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug" eller "trace". Behåll "info" eller "warn" i produktion och använd debug/trace bara vid felsökning.
configHelpTranslations.Optional file path for persisted log out: Valfri filsökväg för persistent loggutskrift utöver eller i stället för konsolloggning. Använd en hanterad skrivbar plats och anpassa retention/rotation till din driftpolicy.
configHelpTranslations.Update channel for git + npm installs (\: Uppdateringskanal för git- och npm-installationer ("stable", "beta" eller "dev").
configHelpTranslations.Check for npm updates when the gateway s: Sök efter npm-uppdateringar när gatewayn startar (standard: true).
configHelpTranslations.TCP port used by the gateway listener fo: TCP-port som gateway-lyssnaren använder för API, kontroll-UI och kanalvända ingressvägar. Använd en dedikerad port och undvik krockar med reverse proxies eller lokala utvecklartjänster.
configHelpTranslations.Gateway operation mode: \"local\" runs c: Gatewayns driftläge: "local" kör kanaler och agentruntime på den här värden, medan "remote" ansluter via fjärrtransport. Behåll "local" om du inte medvetet kör en delad fjärrtopologi.
configHelpTranslations.Control UI hosting settings including en: Inställningar för hur kontroll-UI:t hostas, inklusive aktivering, sökvägar och härdning av browser origin/auth. Håll UI-exponeringen minimal och kombinera med stark auth innan internetexponerade driftsättningar.
configHelpTranslations.OpenTelemetry export settings for traces: OpenTelemetry-exportinställningar för spår, mätvärden och loggar som skickas från gatewaykomponenter. Använd detta när du integrerar med centraliserade observability-backends och distribuerade spårningspipelines.
configHelpTranslations.Groups exec-approval forwarding behavior: Grupperar beteendet för vidarebefordran av exec-godkännanden, inklusive aktivering, routningsläge, filter och explicita mål. Konfigurera här när godkännandefrågor måste nå driftkanaler i stället för bara ursprungstråden.
configHelpTranslations.DM session scoping: \"main\" keeps conti: DM session scoping: "main" keeps continuity, while "per-peer", "per-channel-peer", and "per-account-channel-peer" increase isolation. Use isolated modes for shared inboxes or multi-account deployments.
configHelpTranslations.Gateway auth mode: \"none\", \"token\",: Gateway auth mode: "none", "token", "password", or "trusted-proxy" depending on your edge architecture. Use token/password for direct exposure, and trusted-proxy only behind hardened identity-aware proxies.
configHelpTranslations.Trusted-proxy auth header mapping for up: Trusted-proxy auth header mapping for upstream identity providers that inject user claims. Use only with known proxy CIDRs and strict header allowlists to prevent spoofed identity headers.
configHelpTranslations.Gateway-level tool exposure allow/deny p: Gateway-level tool exposure allow/deny policy that can restrict runtime tool availability independent of agent/tool profiles. Use this for coarse emergency controls and production hardening.
configHelpTranslations.Remote Gateway WebSocket URL (ws:// or w: Remote Gateway WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://).
configHelpTranslations.Bearer token used to authenticate this c: Bearer token used to authenticate this client to a remote gateway in token-auth deployments. Store via secret/env substitution and rotate alongside remote gateway auth changes.
configHelpTranslations.Enables browser capability wiring in the: Enables browser capability wiring in the gateway so browser tools and CDP-driven workflows can run. Disable when browser automation is not needed to reduce surface area and startup work.
configHelpTranslations.Remote CDP websocket URL used to attach: Remote CDP websocket URL used to attach to an externally managed browser instance. Use this for centralized browser hosts and keep URL access restricted to trusted network paths.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit browser executable path when au: Explicit browser executable path when auto-discovery is insufficient for your host environment. Use absolute stable paths so launch behavior stays deterministic across restarts.
configHelpTranslations.Disables Chromium sandbox isolation flag: Disables Chromium sandbox isolation flags for environments where sandboxing fails at runtime. Keep this off whenever possible because process isolation protections are reduced.
configHelpTranslations.Restricts browser mode to attach-only be: Restricts browser mode to attach-only behavior without starting local browser processes. Use this when all browser sessions are externally managed by a remote CDP provider.
configHelpTranslations.Provider map keyed by provider ID contai: Provider map keyed by provider ID containing connection/auth settings and concrete model definitions. Use stable provider keys so references from agents and tooling remain portable across environments.
configHelpTranslations.Base URL for the provider endpoint used: Base URL for the provider endpoint used to serve model requests for that provider entry. Use HTTPS endpoints and keep URLs environment-specific through config templating where needed.
configHelpTranslations.Provider credential used for API-key bas: Provider credential used for API-key based authentication when the provider requires direct key auth. Use secret/env substitution and avoid storing real keys in committed config files.
configHelpTranslations.Enables periodic Bedrock model discovery: Enables periodic Bedrock model discovery and catalog refresh for Bedrock-backed providers. Keep disabled unless Bedrock is actively used and IAM permissions are correctly configured.
configHelpTranslations.Optional provider allowlist filter for B: Optional provider allowlist filter for Bedrock discovery so only selected providers are refreshed. Use this to limit discovery scope in multi-provider environments.
configHelpTranslations.Enables forwarding of exec approval requ: Enables forwarding of exec approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Keep disabled in low-risk setups and enable only when human approval responders need channel-visible prompts.
configHelpTranslations.Controls where approval prompts are sent: Controls where approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths. Use "session" as baseline and expand only when operational workflow requires redundancy.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit delivery targets used when forw: Explicit delivery targets used when forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details. Keep target lists least-privilege and validate each destination before enabling broad forwarding.
configHelpTranslations.Sets base session grouping strategy: \"p: Sets base session grouping strategy: "per-sender" isolates by sender and "global" shares one session per channel context. Keep "per-sender" for safer multi-user behavior unless deliberate shared context is required.
configHelpTranslations.Maps canonical identities to provider-pr: Maps canonical identities to provider-prefixed peer IDs so equivalent users resolve to one DM thread (example: telegram:123456). Use this when the same human appears across multiple channels or accounts.
configHelpTranslations.Lists message triggers that force a sess: Lists message triggers that force a session reset when matched in inbound content. Use sparingly for explicit reset phrases so context is not dropped unexpectedly during normal conversation.
configHelpTranslations.Sets the session storage file path used: Sets the session storage file path used to persist session records across restarts. Use an explicit path only when you need custom disk layout, backup routing, or mounted-volume storage.
configHelpTranslations.Controls cross-session send permissions: Controls cross-session send permissions using allow/deny rules evaluated against channel, chatType, and key prefixes. Use this to fence where session tools can deliver messages in complex environments.
configHelpTranslations.Shared defaults for thread-bound session: Shared defaults for thread-bound session routing behavior across providers that support thread focus workflows. Configure global defaults here and override per channel only when behavior differs.
configHelpTranslations.Automatic session-store maintenance cont: Automatic session-store maintenance controls for pruning age, entry caps, and file rotation behavior. Start in warn mode to observe impact, then enforce once thresholds are tuned.
configHelpTranslations.Remote connection transport: \"direct\": Remote connection transport: "direct" uses configured URL connectivity, while "ssh" tunnels through SSH. Use SSH when you need encrypted tunnel semantics without exposing remote ports.
configHelpTranslations.Live config-reload policy for how edits: Live config-reload policy for how edits are applied and when full restarts are triggered. Keep hybrid behavior for safest operational updates unless debugging reload internals.
configHelpTranslations.TLS certificate and key settings for ter: TLS certificate and key settings for terminating HTTPS directly in the gateway process. Use explicit certificates in production and avoid plaintext exposure on untrusted networks.
configHelpTranslations.Enables TLS termination at the gateway l: Enables TLS termination at the gateway listener so clients connect over HTTPS/WSS directly. Keep enabled for direct internet exposure or any untrusted network boundary.
configHelpTranslations.Auto-generates a local TLS certificate/k: Auto-generates a local TLS certificate/key pair when explicit files are not configured. Use only for local/dev setups and replace with real certificates for production traffic.
configHelpTranslations.Filesystem path to the TLS certificate f: Filesystem path to the TLS certificate file used by the gateway when TLS is enabled. Use managed certificate paths and keep renewal automation aligned with this location.
configHelpTranslations.Filesystem path to the TLS private key f: Filesystem path to the TLS private key file used by the gateway when TLS is enabled. Keep this key file permission-restricted and rotate per your security policy.
configHelpTranslations.Optional CA bundle path for client verif: Optional CA bundle path for client verification or custom trust-chain requirements at the gateway edge. Use this when private PKI or custom certificate chains are part of deployment.
configHelpTranslations.Gateway HTTP API configuration grouping: Gateway HTTP API configuration grouping endpoint toggles and transport-facing API exposure controls. Keep only required endpoints enabled to reduce attack surface.
configHelpTranslations.HTTP endpoint feature toggles under the: HTTP endpoint feature toggles under the gateway API surface for compatibility routes and optional integrations. Enable endpoints intentionally and monitor access patterns after rollout.
configHelpTranslations.Optional HTTP response security headers: Optional HTTP response security headers applied by the gateway process itself. Prefer setting these at your reverse proxy when TLS terminates there.
configHelpTranslations.Value for the Strict-Transport-Security: Value for the Strict-Transport-Security response header. Set only on HTTPS origins that you fully control; use false to explicitly disable.
configHelpTranslations.DANGEROUS toggle that enables Host-heade: DANGEROUS toggle that enables Host-header based origin fallback for Control UI/WebChat websocket checks. This mode is supported when your deployment intentionally relies on Host-header origin policy; explicit gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins remains the recommended hardened default.
configHelpTranslations.Loosens strict browser auth checks for C: Loosens strict browser auth checks for Control UI when you must run a non-standard setup. Keep this off unless you trust your network and proxy path, because impersonation risk is higher.
configHelpTranslations.Sets inactivity window before reset for: Sets inactivity window before reset for idle mode and can also act as secondary guard with daily mode. Use larger values to preserve continuity or smaller values for fresher short-lived threads.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides reset behavior by chat type (d: Overrides reset behavior by chat type (direct, group, thread) when defaults are not sufficient. Use this when group/thread traffic needs different reset cadence than direct messages.
configHelpTranslations.Defines reset policy for direct chats an: Defines reset policy for direct chats and supersedes the base session.reset configuration for that type. Use this as the canonical direct-message override instead of the legacy dm alias.
configHelpTranslations.Deprecated alias for direct reset behavi: Deprecated alias for direct reset behavior kept for backward compatibility with older configs. Use session.resetByType.direct instead so future tooling and validation remain consistent.
configHelpTranslations.Defines reset policy for group chat sess: Defines reset policy for group chat sessions where continuity and noise patterns differ from DMs. Use shorter idle windows for busy groups if context drift becomes a problem.
configHelpTranslations.Defines reset policy for thread-scoped s: Defines reset policy for thread-scoped sessions, including focused channel thread workflows. Use this when thread sessions should expire faster or slower than other chat types.
configHelpTranslations.Provides channel-specific reset override: Provides channel-specific reset overrides keyed by provider/channel id for fine-grained behavior control. Use this only when one channel needs exceptional reset behavior beyond type-level policies.
configHelpTranslations.Controls interval for repeated typing in: Controls interval for repeated typing indicators while replies are being prepared in typing-capable channels. Increase to reduce chatty updates or decrease for more active typing feedback.
configHelpTranslations.CLI-managed install metadata (used by `o: CLI-managed install metadata (used by `openclaw plugins update` to locate install sources).
configHelpTranslations.Original npm spec used for install (if s: Original npm spec used for install (if source is npm).
configHelpTranslations.Original archive/path used for install (: Original archive/path used for install (if any).
configHelpTranslations.Default browser profile name selected wh: Default browser profile name selected when callers do not explicitly choose a profile. Use a stable low-privilege profile as the default to reduce accidental cross-context state use.
configHelpTranslations.Bind IP address for the Chrome extension: Bind IP address for the Chrome extension relay listener. Leave unset for loopback-only access, or set an explicit non-loopback IP such as 0.0.0.0 only when the relay must be reachable across network namespaces (for example WSL2) and the surrounding network is already trusted.
configHelpTranslations.Named browser profile connection map use: Named browser profile connection map used for explicit routing to CDP ports or URLs with optional metadata. Keep profile names consistent and avoid overlapping endpoint definitions.
configHelpTranslations.Per-profile local CDP port used when con: Per-profile local CDP port used when connecting to browser instances by port instead of URL. Use unique ports per profile to avoid connection collisions.
configHelpTranslations.Per-profile CDP websocket URL used for e: Per-profile CDP websocket URL used for explicit remote browser routing by profile name. Use this when profile connections terminate on remote hosts or tunnels.
configHelpTranslations.Per-profile browser driver mode: \"openc: Per-profile browser driver mode: "openclaw" (or legacy "clawd") or "extension" depending on connection/runtime strategy. Use the driver that matches your browser control stack to avoid protocol mismatches.
configHelpTranslations.Per-profile attach-only override that sk: Per-profile attach-only override that skips local browser launch and only attaches to an existing CDP endpoint. Useful when one profile is externally managed but others are locally launched.
configHelpTranslations.Per-profile accent color for visual diff: Per-profile accent color for visual differentiation in dashboards and browser-related UI hints. Use distinct colors for high-signal operator recognition of active profiles.
configHelpTranslations.Enables browser-side evaluate helpers fo: Enables browser-side evaluate helpers for runtime script evaluation capabilities where supported. Keep disabled unless your workflows require evaluate semantics beyond snapshots/navigation.
configHelpTranslations.Default snapshot capture configuration u: Default snapshot capture configuration used when callers do not provide explicit snapshot options. Tune this for consistent capture behavior across channels and automation paths.
configHelpTranslations.Default snapshot extraction mode control: Default snapshot extraction mode controlling how page content is transformed for agent consumption. Choose the mode that balances readability, fidelity, and token footprint for your workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Server-side request forgery guardrail se: Server-side request forgery guardrail settings for browser/network fetch paths that could reach internal hosts. Keep restrictive defaults in production and open only explicitly approved targets.
configHelpTranslations.Legacy alias for browser.ssrfPolicy.dang: Legacy alias for browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork. Prefer the dangerously-named key so risk intent is explicit.
configHelpTranslations.Allows access to private-network address: Allows access to private-network address ranges from browser tooling. Default is enabled for trusted-network operator setups; disable to enforce strict public-only resolution checks.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit hostname allowlist exceptions f: Explicit hostname allowlist exceptions for SSRF policy checks on browser/network requests. Keep this list minimal and review entries regularly to avoid stale broad access.
configHelpTranslations.Legacy/alternate hostname allowlist fiel: Legacy/alternate hostname allowlist field used by SSRF policy consumers for explicit host exceptions. Use stable exact hostnames and avoid wildcard-like broad patterns.
configHelpTranslations.Interval in minutes for automatic channe: Interval in minutes for automatic channel health probing and status updates. Use lower intervals for faster detection, or higher intervals to reduce periodic probe noise.
configHelpTranslations.Tailscale integration settings for Serve: Tailscale integration settings for Serve/Funnel exposure and lifecycle handling on gateway start/exit. Keep off unless your deployment intentionally relies on Tailscale ingress.
configHelpTranslations.Tailscale publish mode: \"off\", \"serve: Tailscale publish mode: "off", "serve", or "funnel" for private or public exposure paths. Use "serve" for tailnet-only access and "funnel" only when public internet reachability is required.
configHelpTranslations.Resets Tailscale Serve/Funnel state on g: Resets Tailscale Serve/Funnel state on gateway exit to avoid stale published routes after shutdown. Keep enabled unless another controller manages publish lifecycle outside the gateway.
configHelpTranslations.Remote gateway connection settings for d: Remote gateway connection settings for direct or SSH transport when this instance proxies to another runtime host. Use remote mode only when split-host operation is intentionally configured.
configHelpTranslations.Global session routing, reset, delivery: Global session routing, reset, delivery policy, and maintenance controls for conversation history behavior. Keep defaults unless you need stricter isolation, retention, or delivery constraints.
configHelpTranslations.Applies a legacy idle reset window in mi: Applies a legacy idle reset window in minutes for session reuse behavior across inactivity gaps. Use this only for compatibility and prefer structured reset policies under session.reset/session.resetByType.
configHelpTranslations.Defines the default reset policy object: Defines the default reset policy object used when no type-specific or channel-specific override applies. Set this first, then layer resetByType or resetByChannel only where behavior must differ.
configHelpTranslations.Selects reset strategy: \"daily\" resets: Selects reset strategy: "daily" resets at a configured hour and "idle" resets after inactivity windows. Keep one clear mode per policy to avoid surprising context turnover patterns.
configHelpTranslations.Sets local-hour boundary (0-23) for dail: Sets local-hour boundary (0-23) for daily reset mode so sessions roll over at predictable times. Use with mode=daily and align to operator timezone expectations for human-readable behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Selects the global memory engine: \"buil: Selects the global memory engine: "builtin" uses OpenClaw memory internals, while "qmd" uses the QMD sidecar pipeline. Keep "builtin" unless you intentionally operate QMD.
configHelpTranslations.Controls citation visibility in replies:: Controls citation visibility in replies: "auto" shows citations when useful, "on" always shows them, and "off" hides them. Keep "auto" for a balanced signal-to-noise default.
configHelpTranslations.Sets the executable path for the `qmd` b: Sets the executable path for the `qmd` binary used by the QMD backend (default: resolved from PATH). Use an explicit absolute path when multiple qmd installs exist or PATH differs across environments.
configHelpTranslations.Routes QMD work through mcporter (MCP ru: Routes QMD work through mcporter (MCP runtime) instead of spawning `qmd` for each call. Use this when cold starts are expensive on large models; keep direct process mode for simpler local setups.
configHelpTranslations.Plugin system controls for enabling exte: Plugin system controls for enabling extensions, constraining load scope, configuring entries, and tracking installs. Keep plugin policy explicit and least-privilege in production environments.
configHelpTranslations.Enable or disable plugin/extension loadi: Enable or disable plugin/extension loading globally during startup and config reload (default: true). Keep enabled only when extension capabilities are required by your deployment.
configHelpTranslations.Optional allowlist of plugin IDs; when s: Optional allowlist of plugin IDs; when set, only listed plugins are eligible to load. Use this to enforce approved extension inventories in controlled environments.
configHelpTranslations.Optional denylist of plugin IDs that are: Optional denylist of plugin IDs that are blocked even if allowlists or paths include them. Use deny rules for emergency rollback and hard blocks on risky plugins.
configHelpTranslations.Plugin loader configuration group for sp: Plugin loader configuration group for specifying filesystem paths where plugins are discovered. Keep load paths explicit and reviewed to avoid accidental untrusted extension loading.
configHelpTranslations.Browser runtime controls for local or re: Browser runtime controls for local or remote CDP attachment, profile routing, and screenshot/snapshot behavior. Keep defaults unless your automation workflow requires custom browser transport settings.
configHelpTranslations.Default accent color used for browser pr: Default accent color used for browser profile/UI cues where colored identity hints are displayed. Use consistent colors to help operators identify active browser profile context quickly.
configHelpTranslations.Forces browser launch in headless mode w: Forces browser launch in headless mode when the local launcher starts browser instances. Keep headless enabled for server environments and disable only when visible UI debugging is required.
configHelpTranslations.Starting local CDP port used for auto-al: Starting local CDP port used for auto-allocated browser profile ports. Increase this when host-level port defaults conflict with other local services.
configHelpTranslations.Default browser profile name selected wh: Default browser profile name selected when callers do not request a specific profile. Keep this stable so scripted browser actions have predictable context.
configHelpTranslations.Service discovery settings for local mDN: Service discovery settings for local mDNS advertisement and optional wide-area presence signaling. Keep discovery scoped to expected networks to avoid leaking service metadata.
configHelpTranslations.mDNS broadcast mode (\"minimal\" default: mDNS broadcast mode ("minimal" default, "full" includes cliPath/sshPort, "off" disables mDNS).
configHelpTranslations.Wide-area discovery configuration group: Wide-area discovery configuration group for exposing discovery signals beyond local-link scopes. Enable only in deployments that intentionally aggregate gateway presence across sites.
configHelpTranslations.Enables wide-area discovery signaling wh: Enables wide-area discovery signaling when your environment needs non-local gateway discovery. Keep disabled unless cross-network discovery is operationally required.
configHelpTranslations.Canvas host settings for serving canvas: Canvas host settings for serving canvas assets and local live-reload behavior used by canvas-enabled workflows. Keep disabled unless canvas-hosted assets are actively used.
configHelpTranslations.Enables the canvas host server process a: Enables the canvas host server process and routes for serving canvas files. Keep disabled when canvas workflows are inactive to reduce exposed local services.
configHelpTranslations.Filesystem root directory served by canv: Filesystem root directory served by canvas host for canvas content and static assets. Use a dedicated directory and avoid broad repo roots for least-privilege file exposure.
configHelpTranslations.TCP port used by the canvas host HTTP se: TCP port used by the canvas host HTTP server when canvas hosting is enabled. Choose a non-conflicting port and align firewall/proxy policy accordingly.
configHelpTranslations.Enables automatic live-reload behavior f: Enables automatic live-reload behavior for canvas assets during development workflows. Keep disabled in production-like environments where deterministic output is preferred.
configHelpTranslations.UI presentation settings for accenting a: UI presentation settings for accenting and assistant identity shown in control surfaces. Use this for branding and readability customization without changing runtime behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Primary accent/seam color used by UI sur: Primary accent/seam color used by UI surfaces for emphasis, badges, and visual identity cues. Use high-contrast values that remain readable across light/dark themes.
configHelpTranslations.Assistant display identity settings for: Assistant display identity settings for name and avatar shown in UI surfaces. Keep these values aligned with your operator-facing persona and support expectations.
configHelpTranslations.Display name shown for the assistant in: Display name shown for the assistant in UI views, chat chrome, and status contexts. Keep this stable so operators can reliably identify which assistant persona is active.
configHelpTranslations.Assistant avatar image source used in UI: Assistant avatar image source used in UI surfaces (URL, path, or data URI depending on runtime support). Use trusted assets and consistent branding dimensions for clean rendering.
configHelpTranslations.Node host controls for features exposed: Node host controls for features exposed from this gateway node to other nodes or clients. Keep defaults unless you intentionally proxy local capabilities across your node network.
configHelpTranslations.Groups browser-proxy settings for exposi: Groups browser-proxy settings for exposing local browser control through node routing. Enable only when remote node workflows need your local browser profiles.
configHelpTranslations.Expose the local browser control server: Expose the local browser control server through node proxy routing so remote clients can use this host's browser capabilities. Keep disabled unless remote automation explicitly depends on it.
configHelpTranslations.Optional allowlist of browser profile na: Optional allowlist of browser profile names exposed through node proxy routing. Leave empty to expose all configured profiles, or use a tight list to enforce least-privilege profile access.
configHelpTranslations.Global audio ingestion settings used bef: Global audio ingestion settings used before higher-level tools process speech or media content. Configure this when you need deterministic transcription behavior for voice notes and clips.
configHelpTranslations.Command-based transcription settings for: Command-based transcription settings for converting audio files into text before agent handling. Keep a simple, deterministic command path here so failures are easy to diagnose in logs.
configHelpTranslations.Executable + args used to transcribe aud: Executable + args used to transcribe audio (first token must be a safe binary/path), for example `["whisper-cli", "--model", "small", "{input}"]`. Prefer a pinned command so runtime environments behave consistently.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum time allowed for the transcripti: Maximum time allowed for the transcription command to finish before it is aborted. Increase this for longer recordings, and keep it tight in latency-sensitive deployments.
configHelpTranslations.Extra node.invoke commands to allow beyo: Extra node.invoke commands to allow beyond the gateway defaults (array of command strings). Enabling dangerous commands here is a security-sensitive override and is flagged by `openclaw security audit`.
configHelpTranslations.Node command names to block even if pres: Node command names to block even if present in node claims or default allowlist (exact command-name matching only, e.g. `system.run`; does not inspect shell text inside that command).
configHelpTranslations.Controls typing behavior timing: \"never: Controls typing behavior timing: "never", "instant", "thinking", or "message" based emission points. Keep conservative modes in high-volume channels to avoid unnecessary typing noise.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum parent-session token count allow: Maximum parent-session token count allowed for thread/session inheritance forking. If the parent exceeds this, OpenClaw starts a fresh thread session instead of forking; set 0 to disable this protection.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides the canonical main session key: Overrides the canonical main session key used for continuity when dmScope or routing logic points to `main`. Use a stable value only if you intentionally need custom session anchoring.
configHelpTranslations.Matches the raw, unnormalized session-ke: Matches the raw, unnormalized session-key prefix for exact full-key policy targeting. Use this when normalized keyPrefix is too broad and you need agent-prefixed or transport-specific precision.
configHelpTranslations.Groups controls for inter-agent session: Groups controls for inter-agent session exchanges, including loop prevention limits on reply chaining. Keep defaults unless you run advanced agent-to-agent automation with strict turn caps.
configHelpTranslations.Max reply-back turns between requester a: Max reply-back turns between requester and target agents during agent-to-agent exchanges (0-5). Use lower values to hard-limit chatter loops and preserve predictable run completion.
configHelpTranslations.Global master switch for thread-bound se: Global master switch for thread-bound session routing features and focused thread delivery behavior. Keep enabled for modern thread workflows unless you need to disable thread binding globally.
configHelpTranslations.Default inactivity window in hours for t: Default inactivity window in hours for thread-bound sessions across providers/channels (0 disables idle auto-unfocus). Default: 24.
configHelpTranslations.Optional hard max age in hours for threa: Optional hard max age in hours for thread-bound sessions across providers/channels (0 disables hard cap). Default: 0.
configHelpTranslations.Determines whether maintenance policies: Determines whether maintenance policies are only reported ("warn") or actively applied ("enforce"). Keep "warn" during rollout and switch to "enforce" after validating safe thresholds.
configHelpTranslations.Removes entries older than this duration: Removes entries older than this duration (for example `30d` or `12h`) during maintenance passes. Use this as the primary age-retention control and align it with data retention policy.
configHelpTranslations.Deprecated age-retention field kept for: Deprecated age-retention field kept for compatibility with legacy configs using day counts. Use session.maintenance.pruneAfter instead so duration syntax and behavior are consistent.
configHelpTranslations.Caps total session entry count retained: Caps total session entry count retained in the store to prevent unbounded growth over time. Use lower limits for constrained environments, or higher limits when longer history is required.
configHelpTranslations.Rotates the session store when file size: Rotates the session store when file size exceeds a threshold such as `10mb` or `1gb`. Use this to bound single-file growth and keep backup/restore operations manageable.
configHelpTranslations.Retention for reset transcript archives: Retention for reset transcript archives (`*.reset.<timestamp>`). Accepts a duration (for example `30d`), or `false` to disable cleanup. Defaults to pruneAfter so reset artifacts do not grow forever.
configHelpTranslations.Optional per-agent sessions-directory di: Optional per-agent sessions-directory disk budget (for example `500mb`). Use this to cap session storage per agent; when exceeded, warn mode reports pressure and enforce mode performs oldest-first cleanup.
configHelpTranslations.Target size after disk-budget cleanup (h: Target size after disk-budget cleanup (high-water mark). Defaults to 80% of maxDiskBytes; set explicitly for tighter reclaim behavior on constrained disks.
configHelpTranslations.Routes QMD through an mcporter daemon in: Routes QMD through an mcporter daemon instead of spawning qmd per request, reducing cold-start overhead for larger models. Keep disabled unless mcporter is installed and configured.
configHelpTranslations.Names the mcporter server target used fo: Names the mcporter server target used for QMD calls (default: qmd). Change only when your mcporter setup uses a custom server name for qmd mcp keep-alive.
configHelpTranslations.Automatically starts the mcporter daemon: Automatically starts the mcporter daemon when mcporter-backed QMD mode is enabled (default: true). Keep enabled unless process lifecycle is managed externally by your service supervisor.
configHelpTranslations.Selects the QMD retrieval path: \"query\: Selects the QMD retrieval path: "query" uses standard query flow, "search" uses search-oriented retrieval, and "vsearch" emphasizes vector retrieval. Keep default unless tuning relevance quality.
configHelpTranslations.Automatically indexes default memory fil: Automatically indexes default memory files (MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md) into QMD collections. Keep enabled unless you want indexing controlled only through explicit custom paths.
configHelpTranslations.Adds custom directories or files to incl: Adds custom directories or files to include in QMD indexing, each with an optional name and glob pattern. Use this for project-specific knowledge locations that are outside default memory paths.
configHelpTranslations.Defines the root location QMD should sca: Defines the root location QMD should scan, using an absolute path or `~`-relative path. Use stable directories so collection identity does not drift across environments.
configHelpTranslations.Filters files under each indexed root us: Filters files under each indexed root using a glob pattern, with default `**/*.md`. Use narrower patterns to reduce noise and indexing cost when directories contain mixed file types.
configHelpTranslations.Sets a stable collection name for an ind: Sets a stable collection name for an indexed path instead of deriving it from filesystem location. Use this when paths vary across machines but you want consistent collection identity.
configHelpTranslations.Additional plugin files or directories s: Additional plugin files or directories scanned by the loader beyond built-in defaults. Use dedicated extension directories and avoid broad paths with unrelated executable content.
configHelpTranslations.Selects which plugins own exclusive runt: Selects which plugins own exclusive runtime slots such as memory so only one plugin provides that capability. Use explicit slot ownership to avoid overlapping providers with conflicting behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Select the active memory plugin by id, o: Select the active memory plugin by id, or "none" to disable memory plugins.
configHelpTranslations.Selects the active context engine plugin: Selects the active context engine plugin by id so one plugin provides context orchestration behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Per-plugin settings keyed by plugin ID i: Per-plugin settings keyed by plugin ID including enablement and plugin-specific runtime configuration payloads. Use this for scoped plugin tuning without changing global loader policy.
configHelpTranslations.Per-plugin enablement override for a spe: Per-plugin enablement override for a specific entry, applied on top of global plugin policy (restart required). Use this to stage plugin rollout gradually across environments.
configHelpTranslations.Per-plugin typed hook policy controls fo: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.
configHelpTranslations.Controls whether this plugin may mutate: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Optional API key field consumed by plugi: Optional API key field consumed by plugins that accept direct key configuration in entry settings. Use secret/env substitution and avoid committing real credentials into config files.
configHelpTranslations.Per-plugin environment variable map inje: Per-plugin environment variable map injected for that plugin runtime context only. Use this to scope provider credentials to one plugin instead of sharing global process environment.
configHelpTranslations.Plugin-defined configuration payload int: Plugin-defined configuration payload interpreted by that plugin's own schema and validation rules. Use only documented fields from the plugin to prevent ignored or invalid settings.
configHelpTranslations.Version recorded at install time (if ava: Version recorded at install time (if available).
configHelpTranslations.Resolved npm package name from the fetch: Resolved npm package name from the fetched artifact.
configHelpTranslations.Resolved npm package version from the fe: Resolved npm package version from the fetched artifact (useful for non-pinned specs).
configHelpTranslations.Resolved exact npm spec (<name>@<version: Resolved exact npm spec (<name>@<version>) from the fetched artifact.
configHelpTranslations.Resolved npm dist integrity hash for the: Resolved npm dist integrity hash for the fetched artifact (if reported by npm).
configHelpTranslations.Resolved npm dist shasum for the fetched: Resolved npm dist shasum for the fetched artifact (if reported by npm).
configHelpTranslations.ISO timestamp when npm package metadata: ISO timestamp when npm package metadata was last resolved for this install record.
configHelpTranslations.Required by default for gateway access (: Required by default for gateway access (unless using Tailscale Serve identity); required for non-loopback binds.
configHelpTranslations.Disables Control UI device identity chec: Disables Control UI device identity checks and relies on token/password only. Use only for short-lived debugging on trusted networks, then turn it off immediately.
configHelpTranslations.Max request body size in bytes for `/v1/: Max request body size in bytes for `/v1/chat/completions` (default: 20MB).
configHelpTranslations.Max number of `image_url` parts accepted: Max number of `image_url` parts accepted from the latest user message (default: 8).
configHelpTranslations.Max cumulative decoded bytes across all: Max cumulative decoded bytes across all `image_url` parts in one request (default: 20MB).
configHelpTranslations.Image fetch/validation controls for Open: Image fetch/validation controls for OpenAI-compatible `image_url` parts.
configHelpTranslations.Allow server-side URL fetches for `image: Allow server-side URL fetches for `image_url` parts (default: false; data URIs remain supported).
configHelpTranslations.Optional hostname allowlist for `image_u: Optional hostname allowlist for `image_url` URL fetches; supports exact hosts and `*.example.com` wildcards.
configHelpTranslations.Allowed MIME types for `image_url` parts: Allowed MIME types for `image_url` parts (case-insensitive list).
configHelpTranslations.Max HTTP redirects allowed when fetching: Max HTTP redirects allowed when fetching `image_url` URLs (default: 3).
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in milliseconds for `image_url`: Timeout in milliseconds for `image_url` URL fetches (default: 10000).
configHelpTranslations.Controls how config edits are applied: \: Controls how config edits are applied: "off" ignores live edits, "restart" always restarts, "hot" applies in-process, and "hybrid" tries hot then restarts if required. Keep "hybrid" for safest routine updates.
configHelpTranslations.Debounce window (ms) before applying con: Debounce window (ms) before applying config changes.
configHelpTranslations.Node browser routing (\"auto\" = pick si: Node browser routing ("auto" = pick single connected browser node, "manual" = require node param, "off" = disable).
configHelpTranslations.Pin browser routing to a specific node i: Pin browser routing to a specific node id or name (optional).
configHelpTranslations.Sets fallback action when no sendPolicy: Sets fallback action when no sendPolicy rule matches: "allow" or "deny". Keep "allow" for simpler setups, or choose "deny" when you require explicit allow rules for every destination.
configHelpTranslations.Ordered allow/deny rules evaluated befor: Ordered allow/deny rules evaluated before the default action, for example `{ action: "deny", match: { channel: "discord" } }`. Put most specific rules first so broad rules do not shadow exceptions.
configHelpTranslations.Defines rule decision as \"allow\" or \": Defines rule decision as "allow" or "deny" when the corresponding match criteria are satisfied. Use deny-first ordering when enforcing strict boundaries with explicit allow exceptions.
configHelpTranslations.Defines optional rule match conditions t: Defines optional rule match conditions that can combine channel, chatType, and key-prefix constraints. Keep matches narrow so policy intent stays readable and debugging remains straightforward.
configHelpTranslations.Matches rule application to a specific c: Matches rule application to a specific channel/provider id (for example discord, telegram, slack). Use this when one channel should permit or deny delivery independently of others.
configHelpTranslations.Matches rule application to chat type (d: Matches rule application to chat type (direct, group, thread) so behavior varies by conversation form. Use this when DM and group destinations require different safety boundaries.
configHelpTranslations.Matches a normalized session-key prefix: Matches a normalized session-key prefix after internal key normalization steps in policy consumers. Use this for general prefix controls, and prefer rawKeyPrefix when exact full-key matching is required.
configHelpTranslations.Matches the raw, unnormalized session-ke: Matches the raw, unnormalized session-key prefix for exact full-key policy targeting. Use this when normalized keyPrefix is too broad and you need agent-specific or literal-key matching.
configHelpTranslations.Indexes session transcripts into QMD so: Indexes session transcripts into QMD so recall can include prior conversation content (experimental, default: false). Enable only when transcript memory is required and you accept larger index churn.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides where sanitized session export: Overrides where sanitized session exports are written before QMD indexing. Use this when default state storage is constrained or when exports must land on a managed volume.
configHelpTranslations.Defines how long exported session files: Defines how long exported session files are kept before automatic pruning, in days (default: unlimited). Set a finite value for storage hygiene or compliance retention policies.
configHelpTranslations.Sets how often QMD refreshes indexes fro: Sets how often QMD refreshes indexes from source content (duration string, default: 5m). Shorter intervals improve freshness but increase background CPU and I/O.
configHelpTranslations.Sets the minimum delay between consecuti: Sets the minimum delay between consecutive QMD refresh attempts in milliseconds (default: 15000). Increase this if frequent file changes cause update thrash or unnecessary background load.
configHelpTranslations.Runs an initial QMD update once during g: Runs an initial QMD update once during gateway startup (default: true). Keep enabled so recall starts from a fresh baseline; disable only when startup speed is more important than immediate freshness.
configHelpTranslations.Blocks startup completion until the init: Blocks startup completion until the initial boot-time QMD sync finishes (default: false). Enable when you need fully up-to-date recall before serving traffic, and keep off for faster boot.
configHelpTranslations.Sets how often QMD recomputes embeddings: Sets how often QMD recomputes embeddings (duration string, default: 60m; set 0 to disable periodic embeds). Lower intervals improve freshness but increase embedding workload and cost.
configHelpTranslations.Sets timeout for QMD maintenance command: Sets timeout for QMD maintenance commands such as collection list/add in milliseconds (default: 30000). Increase when running on slower disks or remote filesystems that delay command completion.
configHelpTranslations.Agent runtime configuration root coverin: Agent runtime configuration root covering defaults and explicit agent entries used for routing and execution context. Keep this section explicit so model/tool behavior stays predictable across multi-agent workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Shared default settings inherited by age: Shared default settings inherited by agents unless overridden per entry in agents.list. Use defaults to enforce consistent baseline behavior and reduce duplicated per-agent configuration.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit list of configured agents with: Explicit list of configured agents with IDs and optional overrides for model, tools, identity, and workspace. Keep IDs stable over time so bindings, approvals, and session routing remain deterministic.
configHelpTranslations.Optional runtime descriptor for this age: Optional runtime descriptor for this agent. Use embedded for default OpenClaw execution or acp for external ACP harness defaults.
configHelpTranslations.Runtime type for this agent: \"embedded\: Runtime type for this agent: "embedded" (default OpenClaw runtime) or "acp" (ACP harness defaults).
configHelpTranslations.ACP runtime defaults for this agent when: ACP runtime defaults for this agent when runtime.type=acp. Binding-level ACP overrides still take precedence per conversation.
configHelpTranslations.Optional ACP harness agent id to use for: Optional ACP harness agent id to use for this OpenClaw agent (for example codex, claude).
configHelpTranslations.Optional ACP backend override for this a: Optional ACP backend override for this agent's ACP sessions (falls back to global acp.backend).
configHelpTranslations.Optional ACP session mode default for th: Optional ACP session mode default for this agent (persistent or oneshot).
configHelpTranslations.Optional default working directory for t: Optional default working directory for this agent's ACP sessions.
configHelpTranslations.Global tool access policy and capability: Global tool access policy and capability configuration across web, exec, media, messaging, and elevated surfaces. Use this section to constrain risky capabilities before broad rollout.
configHelpTranslations.Global tool profile name used to select: Global tool profile name used to select a predefined tool policy baseline before applying allow/deny overrides. Use this for consistent environment posture across agents and keep profile names stable.
configHelpTranslations.Absolute tool allowlist that replaces pr: Absolute tool allowlist that replaces profile-derived defaults for strict environments. Use this only when you intentionally run a tightly curated subset of tool capabilities.
configHelpTranslations.Global tool denylist that blocks listed: Global tool denylist that blocks listed tools even when profile or provider rules would allow them. Use deny rules for emergency lockouts and long-term defense-in-depth.
configHelpTranslations.Policy for allowing agent-to-agent tool: Policy for allowing agent-to-agent tool calls and constraining which target agents can be reached. Keep disabled or tightly scoped unless cross-agent orchestration is intentionally enabled.
configHelpTranslations.Enables the agent_to_agent tool surface: Enables the agent_to_agent tool surface so one agent can invoke another agent at runtime. Keep off in simple deployments and enable only when orchestration value outweighs complexity.
configHelpTranslations.Elevated tool access controls for privil: Elevated tool access controls for privileged command surfaces that should only be reachable from trusted senders. Keep disabled unless operator workflows explicitly require elevated actions.
configHelpTranslations.Enables elevated tool execution path whe: Enables elevated tool execution path when sender and policy checks pass. Keep disabled in public/shared channels and enable only for trusted owner-operated contexts.
configHelpTranslations.Channel provider configurations plus sha: Channel provider configurations plus shared defaults that control access policies, heartbeat visibility, and per-surface behavior. Keep defaults centralized and override per provider only where required.
configHelpTranslations.Telegram channel provider configuration: Telegram channel provider configuration including auth tokens, retry behavior, and message rendering controls. Use this section to tune bot behavior for Telegram-specific API semantics.
configHelpTranslations.Discord channel provider configuration f: Discord channel provider configuration for bot auth, retry policy, streaming, thread bindings, and optional voice capabilities. Keep privileged intents and advanced features disabled unless needed.
configHelpTranslations.Enable Discord thread binding features (: Enable Discord thread binding features (/focus, bound-thread routing/delivery, and thread-bound subagent sessions). Overrides session.threadBindings.enabled when set.
configHelpTranslations.Enable Discord voice channel conversatio: Enable Discord voice channel conversations (default: true). Omit channels.discord.voice to keep voice support disabled for the account.
configHelpTranslations.Enable automatic Discord bot presence up: Enable automatic Discord bot presence updates based on runtime/model availability signals. When enabled: healthy=>online, degraded/unknown=>idle, exhausted/unavailable=>dnd.
configHelpTranslations.Direct message access control (\"pairing: Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.slack.allowFrom=["*"] (legacy: channels.slack.dm.allowFrom).
configHelpTranslations.Message formatting, acknowledgment, queu: Message formatting, acknowledgment, queueing, debounce, and status reaction behavior for inbound/outbound chat flows. Use this section when channel responsiveness or message UX needs adjustment.
configHelpTranslations.Text-to-speech policy for reading agent: Text-to-speech policy for reading agent replies aloud on supported voice or audio surfaces. Keep disabled unless voice playback is part of your operator/user workflow.
configHelpTranslations.Inbound webhook automation surface for m: Inbound webhook automation surface for mapping external events into wake or agent actions in OpenClaw. Keep this locked down with explicit token/session/agent controls before exposing it beyond trusted networks.
configHelpTranslations.Enables the hooks endpoint and mapping e: Enables the hooks endpoint and mapping execution pipeline for inbound webhook requests. Keep disabled unless you are actively routing external events into the gateway.
configHelpTranslations.Base directory for hook transform module: Base directory for hook transform modules referenced by mapping transform.module paths. Use a controlled repo directory so dynamic imports remain reviewable and predictable.
configHelpTranslations.Optional allowlist of skills for this ag: Optional allowlist of skills for this agent (omit = all skills; empty = no skills).
configHelpTranslations.Avatar image path (relative to the agent: Avatar image path (relative to the agent workspace only) or a remote URL/data URL.
configHelpTranslations.Suppress tool error warning payloads dur: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.
configHelpTranslations.Docker network for sandbox browser conta: Docker network for sandbox browser containers (default: openclaw-sandbox-browser). Avoid bridge if you need stricter isolation.
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent override for sandbox browser D: Per-agent override for sandbox browser Docker network.
configHelpTranslations.DANGEROUS break-glass override that allo: DANGEROUS break-glass override that allows sandbox Docker network mode container:<id>. This joins another container namespace and weakens sandbox isolation.
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent DANGEROUS override for contain: Per-agent DANGEROUS override for container namespace joins in sandbox Docker network mode.
configHelpTranslations.Optional CIDR allowlist for container-ed: Optional CIDR allowlist for container-edge CDP ingress (for example 172.21.0.1/32).
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent override for CDP source CIDR a: Per-agent override for CDP source CIDR allowlist.
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent override for tool profile sele: Per-agent override for tool profile selection when one agent needs a different capability baseline. Use this sparingly so policy differences across agents stay intentional and reviewable.
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent additive allowlist for tools o: Per-agent additive allowlist for tools on top of global and profile policy. Keep narrow to avoid accidental privilege expansion on specialized agents.
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent provider-specific tool policy: Per-agent provider-specific tool policy overrides for channel-scoped capability control. Use this when a single agent needs tighter restrictions on one provider than others.
configHelpTranslations.Default workspace path exposed to agent: Default workspace path exposed to agent runtime tools for filesystem context and repo-aware behavior. Set this explicitly when running from wrappers so path resolution stays deterministic.
configHelpTranslations.Max characters of each workspace bootstr: Max characters of each workspace bootstrap file injected into the system prompt before truncation (default: 20000).
configHelpTranslations.Max total characters across all injected: Max total characters across all injected workspace bootstrap files (default: 150000).
configHelpTranslations.Inject agent-visible warning text when b: Inject agent-visible warning text when bootstrap files are truncated: "off", "once" (default), or "always".
configHelpTranslations.Web-tool policy grouping for search/fetc: Web-tool policy grouping for search/fetch providers, limits, and fallback behavior tuning. Keep enabled settings aligned with API key availability and outbound networking policy.
configHelpTranslations.Exec-tool policy grouping for shell exec: Exec-tool policy grouping for shell execution host, security mode, approval behavior, and runtime bindings. Keep conservative defaults in production and tighten elevated execution paths.
configHelpTranslations.Selects execution host strategy for shel: Selects execution host strategy for shell commands, typically controlling local vs delegated execution environment. Use the safest host mode that still satisfies your automation requirements.
configHelpTranslations.Execution security posture selector cont: Execution security posture selector controlling sandbox/approval expectations for command execution. Keep strict security mode for untrusted prompts and relax only for trusted operator workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Node binding configuration for exec tool: Node binding configuration for exec tooling when command execution is delegated through connected nodes. Use explicit node binding only when multi-node routing is required.
configHelpTranslations.Sender allow rules for elevated tools, u: Sender allow rules for elevated tools, usually keyed by channel/provider identity formats. Use narrow, explicit identities so elevated commands cannot be triggered by unintended users.
configHelpTranslations.Allow/deny tool policy applied to spawne: Allow/deny tool policy applied to spawned subagent runtimes for per-subagent hardening. Keep this narrower than parent scope when subagents run semi-autonomous workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Allow/deny tool policy applied when agen: Allow/deny tool policy applied when agents run in sandboxed execution environments. Keep policies minimal so sandbox tasks cannot escalate into unnecessary external actions.
configHelpTranslations.Experimental. Enables apply_patch for Op: Experimental. Enables apply_patch for OpenAI models when allowed by tool policy.
configHelpTranslations.Restrict apply_patch paths to the worksp: Restrict apply_patch paths to the workspace directory (default: true). Set false to allow writing outside the workspace (dangerous).
configHelpTranslations.Optional allowlist of model ids (e.g. \": Optional allowlist of model ids (e.g. "gpt-5.2" or "openai/gpt-5.2").
configHelpTranslations.Enable repetitive tool-call loop detecti: Enable repetitive tool-call loop detection and backoff safety checks (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Prefix text prepended to inbound user me: Prefix text prepended to inbound user messages before they are handed to the agent runtime. Use this sparingly for channel context markers and keep it stable across sessions.
configHelpTranslations.Prefix text prepended to outbound assist: Prefix text prepended to outbound assistant replies before sending to channels. Use for lightweight branding/context tags and avoid long prefixes that reduce content density.
configHelpTranslations.Group-message handling controls includin: Group-message handling controls including mention triggers and history window sizing. Keep mention patterns narrow so group channels do not trigger on every message.
configHelpTranslations.Regex-like patterns used to detect expli: Regex-like patterns used to detect explicit mentions/trigger phrases in group chats. Use precise patterns to reduce false positives in high-volume channels.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of prior group messages l: Maximum number of prior group messages loaded as context per turn for group sessions. Use higher values for richer continuity, or lower values for faster and cheaper responses.
configHelpTranslations.Inbound message queue strategy used to b: Inbound message queue strategy used to buffer bursts before processing turns. Tune this for busy channels where sequential processing or batching behavior matters.
configHelpTranslations.Queue behavior mode: \"steer\", \"follow: Queue behavior mode: "steer", "followup", "collect", "steer-backlog", "steer+backlog", "queue", or "interrupt". Keep conservative modes unless you intentionally need aggressive interruption/backlog semantics.
configHelpTranslations.Per-channel inbound debounce overrides k: Per-channel inbound debounce overrides keyed by provider id in milliseconds. Use this where some providers send message fragments more aggressively than others.
configHelpTranslations.Removes the acknowledgment reaction afte: Removes the acknowledgment reaction after final reply delivery when enabled. Keep enabled for cleaner UX in channels where persistent ack reactions create clutter.
configHelpTranslations.When true, suppress ⚠️ tool-error warnin: When true, suppress ⚠️ tool-error warnings from being shown to the user. The agent already sees errors in context and can retry. Default: false.
configHelpTranslations.Emoji reaction used to acknowledge inbou: Emoji reaction used to acknowledge inbound messages (empty disables).
configHelpTranslations.When to send ack reactions (\"group-ment: When to send ack reactions ("group-mentions", "group-all", "direct", "all", "off", "none"). "off"/"none" disables ack reactions entirely.
configHelpTranslations.HTTP path used by the hooks endpoint (fo: HTTP path used by the hooks endpoint (for example `/hooks`) on the gateway control server. Use a non-guessable path and combine it with token validation for defense in depth.
configHelpTranslations.Shared bearer token checked by hooks ing: Shared bearer token checked by hooks ingress for request authentication before mappings run. Use environment substitution and rotate regularly when webhook endpoints are internet-accessible.
configHelpTranslations.Fallback session key used for hook deliv: Fallback session key used for hook deliveries when a request does not provide one through allowed channels. Use a stable but scoped key to avoid mixing unrelated automation conversations.
configHelpTranslations.Allows callers to supply a session key i: Allows callers to supply a session key in hook requests when true, enabling caller-controlled routing. Keep false unless trusted integrators explicitly need custom session threading.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum accepted webhook payload size in: Maximum accepted webhook payload size in bytes before the request is rejected. Keep this bounded to reduce abuse risk and protect memory usage under bursty integrations.
configHelpTranslations.Named hook preset bundles applied at loa: Named hook preset bundles applied at load time to seed standard mappings and behavior defaults. Keep preset usage explicit so operators can audit which automations are active.
configHelpTranslations.Ordered mapping rules that match inbound: Ordered mapping rules that match inbound hook requests and choose wake or agent actions with optional delivery routing. Use specific mappings first to avoid broad pattern rules capturing everything.
configHelpTranslations.Optional stable identifier for a hook ma: Optional stable identifier for a hook mapping entry used for auditing, troubleshooting, and targeted updates. Use unique IDs so logs and config diffs can reference mappings unambiguously.
configHelpTranslations.Grouping object for mapping match predic: Grouping object for mapping match predicates such as path and source before action routing is applied. Keep match criteria specific so unrelated webhook traffic does not trigger automations.
configHelpTranslations.Path match condition for a hook mapping,: Path match condition for a hook mapping, usually compared against the inbound request path. Use this to split automation behavior by webhook endpoint path families.
configHelpTranslations.Source match condition for a hook mappin: Source match condition for a hook mapping, typically set by trusted upstream metadata or adapter logic. Use stable source identifiers so routing remains deterministic across retries.
configHelpTranslations.Mapping action type: \"wake\" triggers a: Mapping action type: "wake" triggers agent wake flow, while "agent" sends directly to agent handling. Use "agent" for immediate execution and "wake" when heartbeat-driven processing is preferred.
configHelpTranslations.Wake scheduling mode: \"now\" wakes imme: Wake scheduling mode: "now" wakes immediately, while "next-heartbeat" defers until the next heartbeat cycle. Use deferred mode for lower-priority automations that can tolerate slight delay.
configHelpTranslations.Human-readable mapping display name used: Human-readable mapping display name used in diagnostics and operator-facing config UIs. Keep names concise and descriptive so routing intent is obvious during incident review.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit session key override for mappin: Explicit session key override for mapping-delivered messages to control thread continuity. Use stable scoped keys so repeated events correlate without leaking into unrelated conversations.
configHelpTranslations.Template for synthesizing structured map: Template for synthesizing structured mapping input into the final message content sent to the target action path. Keep templates deterministic so downstream parsing and behavior remain stable.
configHelpTranslations.Text-only fallback template used when ri: Text-only fallback template used when rich payload rendering is not desired or not supported. Use this to provide a concise, consistent summary string for chat delivery surfaces.
configHelpTranslations.Controls whether mapping execution resul: Controls whether mapping execution results are delivered back to a channel destination versus being processed silently. Disable delivery for background automations that should not post user-facing output.
configHelpTranslations.Thinking effort override for Gmail-drive: Thinking effort override for Gmail-driven agent runs: "off", "minimal", "low", "medium", or "high". Keep modest defaults for routine inbox automations to control cost and latency.
configHelpTranslations.Internal hook runtime settings for bundl: Internal hook runtime settings for bundled/custom event handlers loaded from module paths. Use this for trusted in-process automations and keep handler loading tightly scoped.
configHelpTranslations.Enables processing for internal hook han: Enables processing for internal hook handlers and configured entries in the internal hook runtime. Keep disabled unless internal hook handlers are intentionally configured.
configHelpTranslations.List of internal event handlers mapping: List of internal event handlers mapping event names to modules and optional exports. Keep handler definitions explicit so event-to-code routing is auditable.
configHelpTranslations.Internal event name that triggers this h: Internal event name that triggers this handler module when emitted by the runtime. Use stable event naming conventions to avoid accidental overlap across handlers.
configHelpTranslations.Safe relative module path for the intern: Safe relative module path for the internal hook handler implementation loaded at runtime. Keep module files in reviewed directories and avoid dynamic path composition.
configHelpTranslations.Optional named export for the internal h: Optional named export for the internal hook handler function when module default export is not used. Set this when one module ships multiple handler entrypoints.
configHelpTranslations.Configured internal hook entry records u: Configured internal hook entry records used to register concrete runtime handlers and metadata. Keep entries explicit and versioned so production behavior is auditable.
configHelpTranslations.Internal hook loader settings controllin: Internal hook loader settings controlling where handler modules are discovered at startup. Use constrained load roots to reduce accidental module conflicts or shadowing.
configHelpTranslations.Additional directories searched for inte: Additional directories searched for internal hook modules beyond default load paths. Keep this minimal and controlled to reduce accidental module shadowing.
configHelpTranslations.Install metadata for internal hook modul: Install metadata for internal hook modules, including source and resolved artifacts for repeatable deployments. Use this as operational provenance and avoid manual drift edits.
configHelpTranslations.Allow bot-authored messages to trigger D: Allow bot-authored messages to trigger Discord replies (default: false). Set "mentions" to only accept bot messages that mention the bot.
configHelpTranslations.Proxy URL for Discord gateway + API requ: Proxy URL for Discord gateway + API requests (app-id lookup and allowlist resolution). Set per account via channels.discord.accounts.<id>.proxy.
configHelpTranslations.Allow WhatsApp to write config in respon: Allow WhatsApp to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Allow Signal to write config in response: Allow Signal to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Signal account identifier (phone/number: Signal account identifier (phone/number handle) used to bind this channel config to a specific Signal identity. Keep this aligned with your linked device/session state.
configHelpTranslations.Allow iMessage to write config in respon: Allow iMessage to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Filesystem path to the iMessage bridge C: Filesystem path to the iMessage bridge CLI binary used for send/receive operations. Set explicitly when the binary is not on PATH in service runtime environments.
configHelpTranslations.Allow Microsoft Teams to write config in: Allow Microsoft Teams to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Map provider -> channel id -> model over: Map provider -> channel id -> model override (values are provider/model or aliases).
configHelpTranslations.Override native commands for Discord (bo: Override native commands for Discord (bool or "auto").
configHelpTranslations.Override native skill commands for Disco: Override native skill commands for Discord (bool or "auto").
configHelpTranslations.Override native commands for Telegram (b: Override native commands for Telegram (bool or "auto").
configHelpTranslations.Override native skill commands for Teleg: Override native skill commands for Telegram (bool or "auto").
configHelpTranslations.Override native commands for Slack (bool: Override native commands for Slack (bool or "auto").
configHelpTranslations.Enables provider batch APIs for embeddin: Enables provider batch APIs for embedding jobs when supported (OpenAI/Gemini), improving throughput on larger index runs. Keep this enabled unless debugging provider batch failures or running very small workloads.
configHelpTranslations.Waits for batch embedding jobs to fully: Waits for batch embedding jobs to fully finish before the indexing operation completes. Keep this enabled for deterministic indexing state; disable only if you accept delayed consistency.
configHelpTranslations.Limits how many embedding batch jobs run: Limits how many embedding batch jobs run at the same time during indexing (default: 2). Increase carefully for faster bulk indexing, but watch provider rate limits and queue errors.
configHelpTranslations.Controls how often the system polls prov: Controls how often the system polls provider APIs for batch job status in milliseconds (default: 2000). Use longer intervals to reduce API chatter, or shorter intervals for faster completion detection.
configHelpTranslations.Sets the maximum wait time for a full em: Sets the maximum wait time for a full embedding batch operation in minutes (default: 60). Increase for very large corpora or slower providers, and lower it to fail fast in automation-heavy flows.
configHelpTranslations.Specifies the local embedding model sour: Specifies the local embedding model source for local memory search, such as a GGUF file path or `hf:` URI. Use this only when provider is `local`, and verify model compatibility before large index rebuilds.
configHelpTranslations.Backup provider used when primary embedd: Backup provider used when primary embeddings fail: "openai", "gemini", "voyage", "mistral", "ollama", "local", or "none". Set a real fallback for production reliability; use "none" only if you prefer explicit failures.
configHelpTranslations.Sets where the SQLite memory index is st: Sets where the SQLite memory index is stored on disk for each agent. Keep the default `~/.openclaw/memory/{agentId}.sqlite` unless you need custom storage placement or backup policy alignment.
configHelpTranslations.Enables the sqlite-vec extension used fo: Enables the sqlite-vec extension used for vector similarity queries in memory search (default: true). Keep this enabled for normal semantic recall; disable only for debugging or fallback-only operation.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides the auto-discovered sqlite-vec: Overrides the auto-discovered sqlite-vec extension library path (`.dylib`, `.so`, or `.dll`). Use this when your runtime cannot find sqlite-vec automatically or you pin a known-good build.
configHelpTranslations.Chunk size in tokens used when splitting: Chunk size in tokens used when splitting memory sources before embedding/indexing. Increase for broader context per chunk, or lower to improve precision on pinpoint lookups.
configHelpTranslations.Token overlap between adjacent memory ch: Token overlap between adjacent memory chunks to preserve context continuity near split boundaries. Use modest overlap to reduce boundary misses without inflating index size too aggressively.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of memory hits returned f: Maximum number of memory hits returned from search before downstream reranking and prompt injection. Raise for broader recall, or lower for tighter prompts and faster responses.
configHelpTranslations.Minimum relevance score threshold for in: Minimum relevance score threshold for including memory results in final recall output. Increase to reduce weak/noisy matches, or lower when you need more permissive retrieval.
configHelpTranslations.Per-channel queue mode overrides keyed b: Per-channel queue mode overrides keyed by provider id (for example telegram, discord, slack). Use this when one channel’s traffic pattern needs different queue behavior than global defaults.
configHelpTranslations.Global queue debounce window in millisec: Global queue debounce window in milliseconds before processing buffered inbound messages. Use higher values to coalesce rapid bursts, or lower values for reduced response latency.
configHelpTranslations.Per-channel debounce overrides for queue: Per-channel debounce overrides for queue behavior keyed by provider id. Use this to tune burst handling independently for chat surfaces with different pacing.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of queued inbound items r: Maximum number of queued inbound items retained before drop policy applies. Keep caps bounded in noisy channels so memory usage remains predictable.
configHelpTranslations.Gmail push integration settings used for: Gmail push integration settings used for Pub/Sub notifications and optional local callback serving. Keep this scoped to dedicated Gmail automation accounts where possible.
configHelpTranslations.Google account identifier used for Gmail: Google account identifier used for Gmail watch/subscription operations in this hook integration. Use a dedicated automation mailbox account to isolate operational permissions.
configHelpTranslations.Optional Gmail label filter limiting whi: Optional Gmail label filter limiting which labeled messages trigger hook events. Keep filters narrow to avoid flooding automations with unrelated inbox traffic.
configHelpTranslations.Google Pub/Sub topic name used by Gmail: Google Pub/Sub topic name used by Gmail watch to publish change notifications for this account. Ensure the topic IAM grants Gmail publish access before enabling watches.
configHelpTranslations.Pub/Sub subscription consumed by the gat: Pub/Sub subscription consumed by the gateway to receive Gmail change notifications from the configured topic. Keep subscription ownership clear so multiple consumers do not race unexpectedly.
configHelpTranslations.Public callback URL Gmail or intermediar: Public callback URL Gmail or intermediaries invoke to deliver notifications into this hook pipeline. Keep this URL protected with token validation and restricted network exposure.
configHelpTranslations.When true, fetch and include email body: When true, fetch and include email body content for downstream mapping/agent processing. Keep false unless body text is required, because this increases payload size and sensitivity.
configHelpTranslations.Allows less-sanitized external Gmail con: Allows less-sanitized external Gmail content to pass into processing when enabled. Keep disabled for safer defaults, and enable only for trusted mail streams with controlled transforms.
