configHelpTranslations.Local callback server settings block for: Local callback server settings block for directly receiving Gmail notifications without a separate ingress layer. Enable only when this process should terminate webhook traffic itself.
configHelpTranslations.Shared secret token required on Gmail pu: Shared secret token required on Gmail push hook callbacks before processing notifications. Use env substitution and rotate if callback endpoints are exposed externally.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum Gmail payload bytes processed pe: Maximum Gmail payload bytes processed per event when includeBody is enabled. Keep conservative limits to reduce oversized message processing cost and risk.
configHelpTranslations.Renewal cadence in minutes for Gmail wat: Renewal cadence in minutes for Gmail watch subscriptions to prevent expiration. Set below provider expiration windows and monitor renew failures in logs.
configHelpTranslations.Bind address for the local Gmail callbac: Bind address for the local Gmail callback HTTP server used when serving hooks directly. Keep loopback-only unless external ingress is intentionally required.
configHelpTranslations.Port for the local Gmail callback HTTP s: Port for the local Gmail callback HTTP server when serve mode is enabled. Use a dedicated port to avoid collisions with gateway/control interfaces.
configHelpTranslations.HTTP path on the local Gmail callback se: HTTP path on the local Gmail callback server where push notifications are accepted. Keep this consistent with subscription configuration to avoid dropped events.
configHelpTranslations.Tailscale exposure mode for Gmail callba: Tailscale exposure mode for Gmail callbacks: "off", "serve", or "funnel". Use "serve" for private tailnet delivery and "funnel" only when public internet ingress is required.
configHelpTranslations.Tailscale exposure configuration block f: Tailscale exposure configuration block for publishing Gmail callbacks through Serve/Funnel routes. Use private tailnet modes before enabling any public ingress path.
configHelpTranslations.Path published by Tailscale Serve/Funnel: Path published by Tailscale Serve/Funnel for Gmail callback forwarding when enabled. Keep it aligned with Gmail webhook config so requests reach the expected handler.
configHelpTranslations.Local service target forwarded by Tailsc: Local service target forwarded by Tailscale Serve/Funnel (for example http://127.0.0.1:8787). Use explicit loopback targets to avoid ambiguous routing.
configHelpTranslations.Optional model override for Gmail-trigge: Optional model override for Gmail-triggered runs when mailbox automations should use dedicated model behavior. Keep unset to inherit agent defaults unless mailbox tasks need specialization.
configHelpTranslations.Allow bot-authored messages to trigger S: Allow bot-authored messages to trigger Slack replies (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Scope for Slack thread history context (: Scope for Slack thread history context ("thread" isolates per thread; "channel" reuses channel history).
configHelpTranslations.If true, Slack thread sessions inherit t: If true, Slack thread sessions inherit the parent channel transcript (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of existing Slack thread: Maximum number of existing Slack thread messages to fetch when starting a new thread session (default: 20, set to 0 to disable).
configHelpTranslations.Bot token from Mattermost System Console: Bot token from Mattermost System Console -> Integrations -> Bot Accounts.
configHelpTranslations.Base URL for your Mattermost server (e.g: Base URL for your Mattermost server (e.g., https://chat.example.com).
configHelpTranslations.Reply to channel messages on mention (\": Reply to channel messages on mention ("oncall"), on trigger chars (">" or "!") ("onchar"), or on every message ("onmessage").
configHelpTranslations.Require @mention in channels before resp: Require @mention in channels before responding (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Default group policy across channels: \": Default group policy across channels: "open", "disabled", or "allowlist". Keep "allowlist" for safer production setups unless broad group participation is intentional.
configHelpTranslations.Default heartbeat visibility settings fo: Default heartbeat visibility settings for status messages emitted by providers/channels. Tune this globally to reduce noisy healthy-state updates while keeping alerts visible.
configHelpTranslations.Shows healthy/OK heartbeat status entrie: Shows healthy/OK heartbeat status entries when true in channel status outputs. Keep false in noisy environments and enable only when operators need explicit healthy confirmations.
configHelpTranslations.Shows degraded/error heartbeat alerts wh: Shows degraded/error heartbeat alerts when true so operator channels surface problems promptly. Keep enabled in production so broken channel states are visible.
configHelpTranslations.Enables concise indicator-style heartbea: Enables concise indicator-style heartbeat rendering instead of verbose status text where supported. Use indicator mode for dense dashboards with many active channels.
configHelpTranslations.Allow Telegram to write config in respon: Allow Telegram to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Telegram bot token used to authenticate: Telegram bot token used to authenticate Bot API requests for this account/provider config. Use secret/env substitution and rotate tokens if exposure is suspected.
configHelpTranslations.Enable Telegram inline button components: Enable Telegram inline button components for supported command and interaction surfaces. Disable if your deployment needs plain-text-only compatibility behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Allow Slack to write config in response: Allow Slack to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Slack bot token used for standard chat a: Slack bot token used for standard chat actions in the configured workspace. Keep this credential scoped and rotate if workspace app permissions change.
configHelpTranslations.Optional Slack user token for workflows: Optional Slack user token for workflows requiring user-context API access beyond bot permissions. Use sparingly and audit scopes because this token can carry broader authority.
configHelpTranslations.When true, treat configured Slack user t: When true, treat configured Slack user token usage as read-only helper behavior where possible. Keep enabled if you only need supplemental reads without user-context writes.
configHelpTranslations.Allow Mattermost to write config in resp: Allow Mattermost to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Allow Discord to write config in respons: Allow Discord to write config in response to channel events/commands (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Discord bot token used for gateway and R: Discord bot token used for gateway and REST API authentication for this provider account. Keep this secret out of committed config and rotate immediately after any leak.
configHelpTranslations.Accent color for Discord component conta: Accent color for Discord component containers (hex). Set per account via channels.discord.accounts.<id>.ui.components.accentColor.
configHelpTranslations.Voice channels to auto-join on startup (: Voice channels to auto-join on startup (list of guildId/channelId entries).
configHelpTranslations.Toggle DAVE end-to-end encryption for Di: Toggle DAVE end-to-end encryption for Discord voice joins (default: true in @discordjs/voice; Discord may require this).
configHelpTranslations.Consecutive decrypt failures before DAVE: Consecutive decrypt failures before DAVE attempts session recovery (passed to @discordjs/voice; default: 24).
configHelpTranslations.Optional TTS overrides for Discord voice: Optional TTS overrides for Discord voice playback (merged with messages.tts).
configHelpTranslations.Enable the Guild Presences privileged in: Enable the Guild Presences privileged intent. Must also be enabled in the Discord Developer Portal. Allows tracking user activities (e.g. Spotify). Default: false.
configHelpTranslations.Enable the Guild Members privileged inte: Enable the Guild Members privileged intent. Must also be enabled in the Discord Developer Portal. Default: false.
configHelpTranslations.Resolve PluralKit proxied messages and t: Resolve PluralKit proxied messages and treat system members as distinct senders.
configHelpTranslations.Optional PluralKit token for resolving p: Optional PluralKit token for resolving private systems or members.
configHelpTranslations.Discord presence activity text (defaults: Discord presence activity text (defaults to custom status).
configHelpTranslations.Discord presence status (online, dnd, id: Discord presence status (online, dnd, idle, invisible).
configHelpTranslations.How often to evaluate Discord auto-prese: How often to evaluate Discord auto-presence state in milliseconds (default: 30000).
configHelpTranslations.Minimum time between actual Discord pres: Minimum time between actual Discord presence update calls in milliseconds (default: 15000). Prevents status spam on noisy state changes.
configHelpTranslations.Optional custom status text while runtim: Optional custom status text while runtime is healthy (online). If omitted, falls back to static channels.discord.activity when set.
Optional custom status text while runtime/model availability is degraded or unknown (idle).
Optional custom status text while runtime detects exhausted/unavailable model quota (dnd). Supports {reason} template placeholder.
configHelpTranslations.Discord presence streaming URL (required: Discord presence streaming URL (required for activityType=1).
configHelpTranslations.Direct message access control (\"pairing: Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.slack.allowFrom=["*"].
configHelpTranslations.Enable the web_search tool (requires a p: Enable the web_search tool (requires a provider API key).
configHelpTranslations.Search provider (\"brave\", \"gemini\",: Search provider ("brave", "gemini", "grok", "kimi", or "perplexity"). Auto-detected from available API keys if omitted.
configHelpTranslations.Brave Search API key (fallback: BRAVE_AP: Brave Search API key (fallback: BRAVE_API_KEY env var).
configHelpTranslations.Brave Search mode: \"web\" (URL results): Brave Search mode: "web" (URL results) or "llm-context" (pre-extracted page content for LLM grounding).
configHelpTranslations.Gemini API key for Google Search groundi: Gemini API key for Google Search grounding (fallback: GEMINI_API_KEY env var).
configHelpTranslations.Grok (xAI) API key (fallback: XAI_API_KE: Grok (xAI) API key (fallback: XAI_API_KEY env var).
configHelpTranslations.Moonshot/Kimi API key (fallback: KIMI_AP: Moonshot/Kimi API key (fallback: KIMI_API_KEY or MOONSHOT_API_KEY env var).
configHelpTranslations.Perplexity or OpenRouter API key (fallba: Perplexity or OpenRouter API key (fallback: PERPLEXITY_API_KEY or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var). Direct Perplexity keys default to the Search API; OpenRouter keys use Sonar chat completions.
configHelpTranslations.Optional Perplexity/OpenRouter chat-comp: Optional Perplexity/OpenRouter chat-completions base URL override. Setting this opts Perplexity into the legacy Sonar/OpenRouter compatibility path.
configHelpTranslations.Optional Sonar/OpenRouter model override: Optional Sonar/OpenRouter model override (default: "perplexity/sonar-pro"). Setting this opts Perplexity into the legacy chat-completions compatibility path.
configHelpTranslations.Enable the web_fetch tool (lightweight H: Enable the web_fetch tool (lightweight HTTP fetch).
configHelpTranslations.Hard cap for web_fetch maxChars (applies: Hard cap for web_fetch maxChars (applies to config and tool calls).
configHelpTranslations.Use Readability to extract main content: Use Readability to extract main content from HTML (fallbacks to basic HTML cleanup).
configHelpTranslations.Enable Firecrawl fallback for web_fetch: Enable Firecrawl fallback for web_fetch (if configured).
configHelpTranslations.Firecrawl API key (fallback: FIRECRAWL_A: Firecrawl API key (fallback: FIRECRAWL_API_KEY env var).
configHelpTranslations.Firecrawl base URL (e.g. https://api.fir: Firecrawl base URL (e.g. https://api.firecrawl.dev or custom endpoint).
configHelpTranslations.When true, Firecrawl returns only the ma: When true, Firecrawl returns only the main content (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Firecrawl maxAge (ms) for cached results: Firecrawl maxAge (ms) for cached results when supported by the API.
configHelpTranslations.Threshold distance to compaction (in tok: Threshold distance to compaction (in tokens) that triggers pre-compaction memory flush execution. Use earlier thresholds for safer persistence, or tighter thresholds for lower flush frequency.
configHelpTranslations.Forces pre-compaction memory flush when: Forces pre-compaction memory flush when transcript file size reaches this threshold (bytes or strings like "2mb"). Use this to prevent long-session hangs even when token counters are stale; set to 0 to disable.
configHelpTranslations.User-prompt template used for the pre-co: User-prompt template used for the pre-compaction memory flush turn when generating memory candidates. Use this only when you need custom extraction instructions beyond the default memory flush behavior.
configHelpTranslations.System-prompt override for the pre-compa: System-prompt override for the pre-compaction memory flush turn to control extraction style and safety constraints. Use carefully so custom instructions do not reduce memory quality or leak sensitive context.
configHelpTranslations.Embedded Pi runner hardening controls fo: Embedded Pi runner hardening controls for how workspace-local Pi settings are trusted and applied in OpenClaw sessions.
configHelpTranslations.How embedded Pi handles workspace-local: How embedded Pi handles workspace-local `.pi/config/settings.json`: "sanitize" (default) strips shellPath/shellCommandPrefix, "ignore" disables project settings entirely, and "trusted" applies project settings as-is.
configHelpTranslations.Minimum delay in ms for custom humanDela: Minimum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 800).
configHelpTranslations.Maximum delay in ms for custom humanDela: Maximum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 2500).
configHelpTranslations.Controls whether heartbeat delivery may: Controls whether heartbeat delivery may target direct/DM chats: "allow" (default) permits DM delivery and "block" suppresses direct-target sends.
configHelpTranslations.Per-agent override for heartbeat direct/: Per-agent override for heartbeat direct/DM delivery policy; use "block" for agents that should only send heartbeat alerts to non-DM destinations.
configHelpTranslations.Direct message access control (\"pairing: Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.discord.allowFrom=["*"] (legacy: channels.discord.dm.allowFrom).
configHelpTranslations.Max retry attempts for outbound Discord: Max retry attempts for outbound Discord API calls (default: 3).
configHelpTranslations.Minimum retry delay in ms for Discord ou: Minimum retry delay in ms for Discord outbound calls.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum retry delay cap in ms for Discor: Maximum retry delay cap in ms for Discord outbound calls.
configHelpTranslations.Jitter factor (0-1) applied to Discord r: Jitter factor (0-1) applied to Discord retry delays.
configHelpTranslations.Soft max line count per Discord message: Soft max line count per Discord message (default: 17).
configHelpTranslations.Optional Discord EventQueue capacity ove: Optional Discord EventQueue capacity override (max queued events before backpressure). Set per account via channels.discord.accounts.<id>.eventQueue.maxQueueSize.
configHelpTranslations.Optional Discord EventQueue concurrency: Optional Discord EventQueue concurrency override (max concurrent handler executions). Set per account via channels.discord.accounts.<id>.eventQueue.maxConcurrency.
configHelpTranslations.Inactivity window in hours for Discord t: Inactivity window in hours for Discord thread-bound sessions (/focus and spawned thread sessions). Set 0 to disable idle auto-unfocus (default: 24). Overrides session.threadBindings.idleHours when set.
configHelpTranslations.Optional hard max age in hours for Disco: Optional hard max age in hours for Discord thread-bound sessions. Set 0 to disable hard cap (default: 0). Overrides session.threadBindings.maxAgeHours when set.
configHelpTranslations.Allow subagent spawns with thread=true t: Allow subagent spawns with thread=true to auto-create and bind Discord threads (default: false; opt-in). Set true to enable thread-bound subagent spawns for this account/channel.
configHelpTranslations.Allow /acp spawn to auto-create and bind: Allow /acp spawn to auto-create and bind Discord threads for ACP sessions (default: false; opt-in). Set true to enable thread-bound ACP spawns for this account/channel.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum accepted video payload size in b: Maximum accepted video payload size in bytes before policy rejection or trimming occurs. Tune this to provider and infrastructure limits to avoid repeated timeout/failure loops.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum characters retained from video u: Maximum characters retained from video understanding output to control prompt growth. Raise for dense scene descriptions and lower when concise summaries are preferred.
configHelpTranslations.Instruction template for video understan: Instruction template for video understanding describing desired summary granularity and focus areas. Keep this stable so output quality remains predictable across model/provider fallbacks.
configHelpTranslations.Attachment eligibility policy for video: Attachment eligibility policy for video analysis, defining which message files can trigger video processing. Keep this explicit in shared channels to prevent accidental large media workloads.
configHelpTranslations.Ordered model preferences specifically f: Ordered model preferences specifically for video understanding before shared media fallback applies. Prioritize models with strong multimodal video support to minimize degraded summaries.
configHelpTranslations.Scope selector controlling when video un: Scope selector controlling when video understanding is attempted across incoming events. Narrow scope in noisy channels, and broaden only where video interpretation is core to workflow.
configHelpTranslations.Restrict filesystem tools (read/write/ed: Restrict filesystem tools (read/write/edit/apply_patch) to the workspace directory (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Controls which sessions can be targeted: Controls which sessions can be targeted by sessions_list/sessions_history/sessions_send. ("tree" default = current session + spawned subagent sessions; "self" = only current; "agent" = any session in the current agent id; "all" = any session; cross-agent still requires tools.agentToAgent).
configHelpTranslations.Legacy override: allow cross-context sen: Legacy override: allow cross-context sends across all providers.
configHelpTranslations.Allow sends to other channels within the: Allow sends to other channels within the same provider (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Allow sends across different providers (: Allow sends across different providers (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Add a visible origin marker when sending: Add a visible origin marker when sending cross-context (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Minimum floor enforced for reserveTokens: Minimum floor enforced for reserveTokens in Pi compaction paths (0 disables the floor guard). Use a non-zero floor to avoid over-aggressive compression under fluctuating token estimates.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum fraction of total context budget: Maximum fraction of total context budget allowed for retained history after compaction (range 0.1-0.9). Use lower shares for more generation headroom or higher shares for deeper historical continuity.
configHelpTranslations.Identifier-preservation policy for compa: Identifier-preservation policy for compaction summaries: "strict" prepends built-in opaque-identifier retention guidance (default), "off" disables this prefix, and "custom" uses identifierInstructions. Keep "strict" unless you have a specific compatibility need.
configHelpTranslations.Custom identifier-preservation instructi: Custom identifier-preservation instruction text used when identifierPolicy="custom". Keep this explicit and safety-focused so compaction summaries do not rewrite opaque IDs, URLs, hosts, or ports.
configHelpTranslations.Number of most recent user/assistant tur: Number of most recent user/assistant turns kept verbatim outside safeguard summarization (default: 3). Raise this to preserve exact recent dialogue context, or lower it to maximize compaction savings.
configHelpTranslations.Optional quality-audit retry settings fo: Optional quality-audit retry settings for safeguard compaction summaries. Leave this disabled unless you explicitly want summary audits and one-shot regeneration on failed checks.
configHelpTranslations.Enables summary quality audits and regen: Enables summary quality audits and regeneration retries for safeguard compaction. Default: false, so safeguard mode alone does not turn on retry behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of regeneration retries a: Maximum number of regeneration retries after a failed safeguard summary quality audit. Use small values to bound extra latency and token cost.
configHelpTranslations.AGENTS.md H2/H3 section names re-injecte: AGENTS.md H2/H3 section names re-injected after compaction so the agent reruns critical startup guidance. Leave unset to use "Session Startup"/"Red Lines" with legacy fallback to "Every Session"/"Safety"; set to [] to disable reinjection entirely.
configHelpTranslations.Optional provider/model override used on: Optional provider/model override used only for compaction summarization. Set this when you want compaction to run on a different model than the session default, and leave it unset to keep using the primary agent model.
configHelpTranslations.Pre-compaction memory flush settings tha: Pre-compaction memory flush settings that run an agentic memory write before heavy compaction. Keep enabled for long sessions so salient context is persisted before aggressive trimming.
configHelpTranslations.Enables pre-compaction memory flush befo: Enables pre-compaction memory flush before the runtime performs stronger history reduction near token limits. Keep enabled unless you intentionally disable memory side effects in constrained environments.
configHelpTranslations.Override Node autoSelectFamily for Teleg: Override Node autoSelectFamily for Telegram (true=enable, false=disable).
configHelpTranslations.Max seconds before Telegram API requests: Max seconds before Telegram API requests are aborted (default: 500 per grammY).
configHelpTranslations.Enable Telegram conversation binding fea: Enable Telegram conversation binding features (/focus, /unfocus, /agents, and /session idle|max-age). Overrides session.threadBindings.enabled when set.
configHelpTranslations.Inactivity window in hours for Telegram: Inactivity window in hours for Telegram bound sessions. Set 0 to disable idle auto-unfocus (default: 24). Overrides session.threadBindings.idleHours when set.
configHelpTranslations.Optional hard max age in hours for Teleg: Optional hard max age in hours for Telegram bound sessions. Set 0 to disable hard cap (default: 0). Overrides session.threadBindings.maxAgeHours when set.
configHelpTranslations.Allow subagent spawns with thread=true t: Allow subagent spawns with thread=true to auto-bind Telegram current conversations when supported.
configHelpTranslations.Allow ACP spawns with thread=true to aut: Allow ACP spawns with thread=true to auto-bind Telegram current conversations when supported.
configHelpTranslations.Direct message access control (\"pairing: Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.whatsapp.allowFrom=["*"].
configHelpTranslations.Same-phone setup (bot uses your personal: Same-phone setup (bot uses your personal WhatsApp number).
configHelpTranslations.Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid: Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid consecutive messages from the same sender (0 to disable).
configHelpTranslations.Direct message access control (\"pairing: Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.signal.allowFrom=["*"].
Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.imessage.allowFrom=["*"].
Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.bluebubbles.allowFrom=["*"].
Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.discord.allowFrom=["*"].
configHelpTranslations.Ordered fallback models (provider/model): Ordered fallback models (provider/model). Used when the primary model fails.
configHelpTranslations.Optional image model (provider/model) us: Optional image model (provider/model) used when the primary model lacks image input.
configHelpTranslations.Optional PDF model (provider/model) for: Optional PDF model (provider/model) for the PDF analysis tool. Defaults to imageModel, then session model.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum PDF file size in megabytes for t: Maximum PDF file size in megabytes for the PDF tool (default: 10).
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of PDF pages to process f: Maximum number of PDF pages to process for the PDF tool (default: 20).
configHelpTranslations.Max image side length in pixels when san: Max image side length in pixels when sanitizing transcript/tool-result image payloads (default: 1200).
configHelpTranslations.Optional CLI backends for text-only fall: Optional CLI backends for text-only fallback (claude-cli, etc.).
configHelpTranslations.Compaction tuning for when context nears: Compaction tuning for when context nears token limits, including history share, reserve headroom, and pre-compaction memory flush behavior. Use this when long-running sessions need stable continuity under tight context windows.
configHelpTranslations.Compaction strategy mode: \"default\" us: Compaction strategy mode: "default" uses baseline behavior, while "safeguard" applies stricter guardrails to preserve recent context. Keep "default" unless you observe aggressive history loss near limit boundaries.
configHelpTranslations.Token headroom reserved for reply genera: Token headroom reserved for reply generation and tool output after compaction runs. Use higher reserves for verbose/tool-heavy sessions, and lower reserves when maximizing retained history matters more.
configHelpTranslations.Minimum token budget preserved from the: Minimum token budget preserved from the most recent conversation window during compaction. Use higher values to protect immediate context continuity and lower values to keep more long-tail history.
configHelpTranslations.Override native skill commands for Slack: Override native skill commands for Slack (bool or "auto").
configHelpTranslations.Unified Slack stream preview mode: \"off: Unified Slack stream preview mode: "off" | "partial" | "block" | "progress". Legacy boolean/streamMode keys are auto-mapped.
configHelpTranslations.Enable native Slack text streaming (chat: Enable native Slack text streaming (chat.startStream/chat.appendStream/chat.stopStream) when channels.slack.streaming is partial (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Legacy Slack preview mode alias (replace: Legacy Slack preview mode alias (replace | status_final | append); auto-migrated to channels.slack.streaming.
configHelpTranslations.Additional Telegram bot menu commands (m: Additional Telegram bot menu commands (merged with native; conflicts ignored).
configHelpTranslations.Direct message access control (\"pairing: Direct message access control ("pairing" recommended). "open" requires channels.telegram.allowFrom=["*"].
configHelpTranslations.Unified Telegram stream preview mode: \": Unified Telegram stream preview mode: "off" | "partial" | "block" | "progress" (default: "partial"). "progress" maps to "partial" on Telegram. Legacy boolean/streamMode keys are auto-mapped.
configHelpTranslations.Unified Discord stream preview mode: \"o: Unified Discord stream preview mode: "off" | "partial" | "block" | "progress". "progress" maps to "partial" on Discord. Legacy boolean/streamMode keys are auto-mapped.
configHelpTranslations.Legacy Discord preview mode alias (off |: Legacy Discord preview mode alias (off | partial | block); auto-migrated to channels.discord.streaming.
configHelpTranslations.Minimum chars before emitting a Discord: Minimum chars before emitting a Discord stream preview update when channels.discord.streaming="block" (default: 200).
configHelpTranslations.Target max size for a Discord stream pre: Target max size for a Discord stream preview chunk when channels.discord.streaming="block" (default: 800; clamped to channels.discord.textChunkLimit).
configHelpTranslations.Preferred breakpoints for Discord draft: Preferred breakpoints for Discord draft chunks (paragraph | newline | sentence). Default: paragraph.
configHelpTranslations.Max retry attempts for outbound Telegram: Max retry attempts for outbound Telegram API calls (default: 3).
configHelpTranslations.Minimum retry delay in ms for Telegram o: Minimum retry delay in ms for Telegram outbound calls.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum retry delay cap in ms for Telegr: Maximum retry delay cap in ms for Telegram outbound calls.
configHelpTranslations.Jitter factor (0-1) applied to Telegram: Jitter factor (0-1) applied to Telegram retry delays.
configHelpTranslations.Delay in milliseconds before showing an: Delay in milliseconds before showing an in-progress notice after an exec approval is granted. Increase to reduce flicker for fast commands, or lower for quicker operator feedback.
configHelpTranslations.Enable automatic link understanding pre-: Enable automatic link understanding pre-processing so URLs can be summarized before agent reasoning. Keep enabled for richer context, and disable when strict minimal processing is required.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of links expanded per tur: Maximum number of links expanded per turn during link understanding. Use lower values to control latency/cost in chatty threads and higher values when multi-link context is critical.
configHelpTranslations.Per-link understanding timeout budget in: Per-link understanding timeout budget in seconds before unresolved links are skipped. Keep this bounded to avoid long stalls when external sites are slow or unreachable.
configHelpTranslations.Preferred model list for link understand: Preferred model list for link understanding tasks, evaluated in order as fallbacks when supported. Use lightweight models first for routine summarization and heavier models only when needed.
configHelpTranslations.Controls when link understanding runs re: Controls when link understanding runs relative to conversation context and message type. Keep scope conservative to avoid unnecessary fetches on messages where links are not actionable.
configHelpTranslations.Shared fallback model list used by media: Shared fallback model list used by media understanding tools when modality-specific model lists are not set. Keep this aligned with available multimodal providers to avoid runtime fallback churn.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum number of concurrent media under: Maximum number of concurrent media understanding operations per turn across image, audio, and video tasks. Lower this in resource-constrained deployments to prevent CPU/network saturation.
configHelpTranslations.Enable image understanding so attached o: Enable image understanding so attached or referenced images can be interpreted into textual context. Disable if you need text-only operation or want to avoid image-processing cost.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum accepted image payload size in b: Maximum accepted image payload size in bytes before the item is skipped or truncated by policy. Keep limits realistic for your provider caps and infrastructure bandwidth.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum characters returned from image u: Maximum characters returned from image understanding output after model response normalization. Use tighter limits to reduce prompt bloat and larger limits for detail-heavy OCR tasks.
configHelpTranslations.Instruction template used for image unde: Instruction template used for image understanding requests to shape extraction style and detail level. Keep prompts deterministic so outputs stay consistent across turns and channels.
configHelpTranslations.Attachment handling policy for image inp: Attachment handling policy for image inputs, including which message attachments qualify for image analysis. Use restrictive settings in untrusted channels to reduce unexpected processing.
configHelpTranslations.Ordered model preferences specifically f: Ordered model preferences specifically for image understanding when you want to override shared media models. Put the most reliable multimodal model first to reduce fallback attempts.
configHelpTranslations.Scope selector for when image understand: Scope selector for when image understanding is attempted (for example only explicit requests versus broader auto-detection). Keep narrow scope in busy channels to control token and API spend.
configHelpTranslations.Enable video understanding so clips can: Enable video understanding so clips can be summarized into text for downstream reasoning and responses. Disable when processing video is out of policy or too expensive for your deployment.
configHelpTranslations.Combines BM25 keyword matching with vect: Combines BM25 keyword matching with vector similarity for better recall on mixed exact + semantic queries. Keep enabled unless you are isolating ranking behavior for troubleshooting.
configHelpTranslations.Controls how strongly semantic similarit: Controls how strongly semantic similarity influences hybrid ranking (0-1). Increase when paraphrase matching matters more than exact terms; decrease for stricter keyword emphasis.
configHelpTranslations.Controls how strongly BM25 keyword relev: Controls how strongly BM25 keyword relevance influences hybrid ranking (0-1). Increase for exact-term matching; decrease when semantic matches should rank higher.
configHelpTranslations.Expands the candidate pool before rerank: Expands the candidate pool before reranking (default: 4). Raise this for better recall on noisy corpora, but expect more compute and slightly slower searches.
configHelpTranslations.Adds MMR reranking to diversify results: Adds MMR reranking to diversify results and reduce near-duplicate snippets in a single answer window. Enable when recall looks repetitive; keep off for strict score ordering.
configHelpTranslations.Sets MMR relevance-vs-diversity balance: Sets MMR relevance-vs-diversity balance (0 = most diverse, 1 = most relevant, default: 0.7). Lower values reduce repetition; higher values keep tightly relevant but may duplicate.
configHelpTranslations.Applies recency decay so newer memory ca: Applies recency decay so newer memory can outrank older memory when scores are close. Enable when timeliness matters; keep off for timeless reference knowledge.
configHelpTranslations.Controls how fast older memory loses ran: Controls how fast older memory loses rank when temporal decay is enabled (half-life in days, default: 30). Lower values prioritize recent context more aggressively.
configHelpTranslations.Caches computed chunk embeddings in SQLi: Caches computed chunk embeddings in SQLite so reindexing and incremental updates run faster (default: true). Keep this enabled unless investigating cache correctness or minimizing disk usage.
configHelpTranslations.Sets a best-effort upper bound on cached: Sets a best-effort upper bound on cached embeddings kept in SQLite for memory search. Use this when controlling disk growth matters more than peak reindex speed.
configHelpTranslations.Triggers a memory index sync when a sess: Triggers a memory index sync when a session starts so early turns see fresh memory content. Keep enabled when startup freshness matters more than initial turn latency.
configHelpTranslations.Uses lazy sync by scheduling reindex on: Uses lazy sync by scheduling reindex on search after content changes are detected. Keep enabled for lower idle overhead, or disable if you require pre-synced indexes before any query.
configHelpTranslations.Watches memory files and schedules index: Watches memory files and schedules index updates from file-change events (chokidar). Enable for near-real-time freshness; disable on very large workspaces if watch churn is too noisy.
configHelpTranslations.Debounce window in milliseconds for coal: Debounce window in milliseconds for coalescing rapid file-watch events before reindex runs. Increase to reduce churn on frequently-written files, or lower for faster freshness.
configHelpTranslations.Requires at least this many newly append: Requires at least this many newly appended bytes before session transcript changes trigger reindex (default: 100000). Increase to reduce frequent small reindexes, or lower for faster transcript freshness.
configHelpTranslations.Requires at least this many appended tra: Requires at least this many appended transcript messages before reindex is triggered (default: 50). Lower this for near-real-time transcript recall, or raise it to reduce indexing churn.
configHelpTranslations.Optional repository root shown in the sy: Optional repository root shown in the system prompt runtime line (overrides auto-detect).
configHelpTranslations.Timezone for message envelopes (\"utc\",: Timezone for message envelopes ("utc", "local", "user", or an IANA timezone string).
configHelpTranslations.Include absolute timestamps in message e: Include absolute timestamps in message envelopes ("on" or "off").
configHelpTranslations.Include elapsed time in message envelope: Include elapsed time in message envelopes ("on" or "off").
configHelpTranslations.Configured model catalog (keys are full: Configured model catalog (keys are full provider/model IDs).
configHelpTranslations.Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/: Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).
configHelpTranslations.Master toggle for memory search indexing: Master toggle for memory search indexing and retrieval behavior on this agent profile. Keep enabled for semantic recall, and disable when you want fully stateless responses.
configHelpTranslations.Chooses which sources are indexed: \"mem: Chooses which sources are indexed: "memory" reads MEMORY.md + memory files, and "sessions" includes transcript history. Keep ["memory"] unless you need recall from prior chat transcripts.
configHelpTranslations.Adds extra directories or .md files to t: Adds extra directories or .md files to the memory index beyond default memory files. Use this when key reference docs live elsewhere in your repo; keep paths small and intentional to avoid noisy recall.
configHelpTranslations.Indexes session transcripts into memory: Indexes session transcripts into memory search so responses can reference prior chat turns. Keep this off unless transcript recall is needed, because indexing cost and storage usage both increase.
configHelpTranslations.Selects the embedding backend used to bu: Selects the embedding backend used to build/query memory vectors: "openai", "gemini", "voyage", "mistral", "ollama", or "local". Keep your most reliable provider here and configure fallback for resilience.
configHelpTranslations.Embedding model override used by the sel: Embedding model override used by the selected memory provider when a non-default model is required. Set this only when you need explicit recall quality/cost tuning beyond provider defaults.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides the embedding API endpoint, su: Overrides the embedding API endpoint, such as an OpenAI-compatible proxy or custom Gemini base URL. Use this only when routing through your own gateway or vendor endpoint; keep provider defaults otherwise.
configHelpTranslations.Supplies a dedicated API key for remote: Supplies a dedicated API key for remote embedding calls used by memory indexing and query-time embeddings. Use this when memory embeddings should use different credentials than global defaults or environment variables.
configHelpTranslations.Adds custom HTTP headers to remote embed: Adds custom HTTP headers to remote embedding requests, merged with provider defaults. Use this for proxy auth and tenant routing headers, and keep values minimal to avoid leaking sensitive metadata.
configHelpTranslations.Warning threshold for repetitive pattern: Warning threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 10).
configHelpTranslations.Critical threshold for repetitive patter: Critical threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 20).
configHelpTranslations.Enable generic repeated same-tool/same-p: Enable generic repeated same-tool/same-params loop detection (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Enable known poll tool no-progress loop: Enable known poll tool no-progress loop detection (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.When true (default), backgrounded exec s: When true (default), backgrounded exec sessions on exit and node exec lifecycle events enqueue a system event and request a heartbeat.
configHelpTranslations.When true, successful backgrounded exec: When true, successful backgrounded exec exits with empty output still enqueue a completion system event (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Directories to prepend to PATH for exec: Directories to prepend to PATH for exec runs (gateway/sandbox).
configHelpTranslations.Allow stdin-only safe binaries to run wi: Allow stdin-only safe binaries to run without explicit allowlist entries.
configHelpTranslations.Additional explicit directories trusted: Additional explicit directories trusted for safe-bin path checks (PATH entries are never auto-trusted).
configHelpTranslations.Optional per-binary safe-bin profiles (p: Optional per-binary safe-bin profiles (positional limits + allowed/denied flags).
configHelpTranslations.Extra tool allowlist entries merged on t: Extra tool allowlist entries merged on top of the selected tool profile and default policy. Keep this list small and explicit so audits can quickly identify intentional policy exceptions.
configHelpTranslations.Per-provider tool allow/deny overrides k: Per-provider tool allow/deny overrides keyed by channel/provider ID to tailor capabilities by surface. Use this when one provider needs stricter controls than global tool policy.
configHelpTranslations.Drop strategy when queue cap is exceeded: Drop strategy when queue cap is exceeded: "old", "new", or "summarize". Use summarize when preserving intent matters, or old/new when deterministic dropping is preferred.
configHelpTranslations.Direct inbound debounce settings used be: Direct inbound debounce settings used before queue/turn processing starts. Configure this for provider-specific rapid message bursts from the same sender.
configHelpTranslations.Lifecycle status reactions that update t: Lifecycle status reactions that update the emoji on the trigger message as the agent progresses (queued → thinking → tool → done/error).
configHelpTranslations.Enable lifecycle status reactions for Te: Enable lifecycle status reactions for Telegram. When enabled, the ack reaction becomes the initial 'queued' state and progresses through thinking, tool, done/error automatically. Default: false.
Override default status reaction emojis. Keys: thinking, tool, coding, web, done, error, stallSoft, stallHard. Must be valid Telegram reaction emojis.
configHelpTranslations.Override default timing. Keys: debounceM: Override default timing. Keys: debounceMs (700), stallSoftMs (25000), stallHardMs (60000), doneHoldMs (1500), errorHoldMs (2500).
configHelpTranslations.Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid: Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid inbound messages from the same sender (0 to disable).
configHelpTranslations.Slack channel provider configuration for: Slack channel provider configuration for bot/app tokens, streaming behavior, and DM policy controls. Keep token handling and thread behavior explicit to avoid noisy workspace interactions.
configHelpTranslations.WhatsApp channel provider configuration: WhatsApp channel provider configuration for access policy and message batching behavior. Use this section to tune responsiveness and direct-message routing safety for WhatsApp chats.
configHelpTranslations.Signal channel provider configuration in: Signal channel provider configuration including account identity and DM policy behavior. Keep account mapping explicit so routing remains stable across multi-device setups.
configHelpTranslations.iMessage channel provider configuration: iMessage channel provider configuration for CLI integration and DM access policy handling. Use explicit CLI paths when runtime environments have non-standard binary locations.
configHelpTranslations.BlueBubbles channel provider configurati: BlueBubbles channel provider configuration used for Apple messaging bridge integrations. Keep DM policy aligned with your trusted sender model in shared deployments.
configHelpTranslations.Microsoft Teams channel provider configu: Microsoft Teams channel provider configuration and provider-specific policy toggles. Use this section to isolate Teams behavior from other enterprise chat providers.
configHelpTranslations.Mattermost channel provider configuratio: Mattermost channel provider configuration for bot credentials, base URL, and message trigger modes. Keep mention/trigger rules strict in high-volume team channels.
configHelpTranslations.IRC channel provider configuration and c: IRC channel provider configuration and compatibility settings for classic IRC transport workflows. Use this section when bridging legacy chat infrastructure into OpenClaw.
configHelpTranslations.Default channel behavior applied across: Default channel behavior applied across providers when provider-specific settings are not set. Use this to enforce consistent baseline policy before per-provider tuning.
configHelpTranslations.When true, mapping content may include l: When true, mapping content may include less-sanitized external payload data in generated messages. Keep false by default and enable only for trusted sources with reviewed transform logic.
configHelpTranslations.Delivery channel override for mapping ou: Delivery channel override for mapping outputs (for example "last", "telegram", "discord", "slack", "signal", "imessage", or "msteams"). Keep channel overrides explicit to avoid accidental cross-channel sends.
configHelpTranslations.Destination identifier inside the select: Destination identifier inside the selected channel when mapping replies should route to a fixed target. Verify provider-specific destination formats before enabling production mappings.
configHelpTranslations.Optional model override for mapping-trig: Optional model override for mapping-triggered runs when automation should use a different model than agent defaults. Use this sparingly so behavior remains predictable across mapping executions.
configHelpTranslations.Optional thinking-effort override for ma: Optional thinking-effort override for mapping-triggered runs to tune latency versus reasoning depth. Keep low or minimal for high-volume hooks unless deeper reasoning is clearly required.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum runtime allowed for mapping acti: Maximum runtime allowed for mapping action execution before timeout handling applies. Use tighter limits for high-volume webhook sources to prevent queue pileups.
configHelpTranslations.Transform configuration block defining m: Transform configuration block defining module/export preprocessing before mapping action handling. Use transforms only from reviewed code paths and keep behavior deterministic for repeatable automation.
configHelpTranslations.Relative transform module path loaded fr: Relative transform module path loaded from hooks.transformsDir to rewrite incoming payloads before delivery. Keep modules local, reviewed, and free of path traversal patterns.
configHelpTranslations.Named export to invoke from the transfor: Named export to invoke from the transform module; defaults to module default export when omitted. Set this when one file hosts multiple transform handlers.
configHelpTranslations.ISO timestamp for when the setup wizard: ISO timestamp for when the setup wizard most recently completed on this host. Use this to confirm onboarding recency during support and operational audits.
configHelpTranslations.OpenClaw version recorded at the time of: OpenClaw version recorded at the time of the most recent wizard run on this config. Use this when diagnosing behavior differences across version-to-version onboarding changes.
configHelpTranslations.Source commit identifier recorded for th: Source commit identifier recorded for the last wizard execution in development builds. Use this to correlate onboarding behavior with exact source state during debugging.
configHelpTranslations.Command invocation recorded for the late: Command invocation recorded for the latest wizard run to preserve execution context. Use this to reproduce onboarding steps when verifying setup regressions.
configHelpTranslations.Wizard execution mode recorded as \"loca: Wizard execution mode recorded as "local" or "remote" for the most recent onboarding flow. Use this to understand whether setup targeted direct local runtime or remote gateway topology.
configHelpTranslations.Cache-trace logging settings for observi: Cache-trace logging settings for observing cache decisions and payload context in embedded runs. Enable this temporarily for debugging and disable afterward to reduce sensitive log footprint.
configHelpTranslations.Console-specific log threshold: \"silent: Console-specific log threshold: "silent", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug", or "trace" for terminal output control. Use this to keep local console quieter while retaining richer file logging if needed.
configHelpTranslations.Console output format style: \"pretty\",: Console output format style: "pretty", "compact", or "json" based on operator and ingestion needs. Use json for machine parsing pipelines and pretty/compact for human-first terminal workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Sensitive redaction mode: \"off\" disabl: Sensitive redaction mode: "off" disables built-in masking, while "tools" redacts sensitive tool/config payload fields. Keep "tools" in shared logs unless you have isolated secure log sinks.
configHelpTranslations.Additional custom redact regex patterns: Additional custom redact regex patterns applied to log output before emission/storage. Use this to mask org-specific tokens and identifiers not covered by built-in redaction rules.
configHelpTranslations.CLI startup banner controls for title/ve: CLI startup banner controls for title/version line and tagline style behavior. Keep banner enabled for fast version/context checks, then tune tagline mode to your preferred noise level.
configHelpTranslations.Controls tagline style in the CLI startu: Controls tagline style in the CLI startup banner: "random" (default) picks from the rotating tagline pool, "default" always shows the neutral default tagline, and "off" hides tagline text while keeping the banner version line.
configHelpTranslations.Enable background auto-update for packag: Enable background auto-update for package installs (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Minimum delay before stable-channel auto: Minimum delay before stable-channel auto-apply starts (default: 6).
configHelpTranslations.Extra stable-channel rollout spread wind: Extra stable-channel rollout spread window in hours (default: 12).
configHelpTranslations.How often beta-channel checks run in hou: How often beta-channel checks run in hours (default: 1).
configHelpTranslations.Network bind profile: \"auto\", \"lan\",: Network bind profile: "auto", "lan", "loopback", "custom", or "tailnet" to control interface exposure. Keep "loopback" or "auto" for safest local operation unless external clients must connect.
Explicit bind host/IP used when gateway.bind is set to custom for manual interface targeting. Use a precise address and avoid wildcard binds unless external exposure is required.
configHelpTranslations.Enables serving the gateway Control UI f: Enables serving the gateway Control UI from the gateway HTTP process when true. Keep enabled for local administration, and disable when an external control surface replaces it.
configHelpTranslations.Authentication policy for gateway HTTP/W: Authentication policy for gateway HTTP/WebSocket access including mode, credentials, trusted-proxy behavior, and rate limiting. Keep auth enabled for every non-loopback deployment.
configHelpTranslations.Allows trusted Tailscale identity paths: Allows trusted Tailscale identity paths to satisfy gateway auth checks when configured. Use this only when your tailnet identity posture is strong and operator workflows depend on it.
configHelpTranslations.Login/auth attempt throttling controls t: Login/auth attempt throttling controls to reduce credential brute-force risk at the gateway boundary. Keep enabled in exposed environments and tune thresholds to your traffic baseline.
configHelpTranslations.CIDR/IP allowlist of upstream proxies pe: CIDR/IP allowlist of upstream proxies permitted to provide forwarded client identity headers. Keep this list narrow so untrusted hops cannot impersonate users.
configHelpTranslations.Enables x-real-ip fallback when x-forwar: Enables x-real-ip fallback when x-forwarded-for is missing in proxy scenarios. Keep disabled unless your ingress stack requires this compatibility behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit gateway-level tool allowlist wh: Explicit gateway-level tool allowlist when you want a narrow set of tools available at runtime. Use this for locked-down environments where tool scope must be tightly controlled.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit gateway-level tool denylist to: Explicit gateway-level tool denylist to block risky tools even if lower-level policies allow them. Use deny rules for emergency response and defense-in-depth hardening.
configHelpTranslations.Interval in minutes for automatic channe: Interval in minutes for automatic channel health checks (default: 5).
configHelpTranslations.Password credential used for remote gate: Password credential used for remote gateway authentication when password mode is enabled. Keep this secret managed externally and avoid plaintext values in committed config.
configHelpTranslations.Expected sha256 TLS fingerprint for the: Expected sha256 TLS fingerprint for the remote gateway (pin to avoid MITM).
configHelpTranslations.Remote gateway over SSH (tunnels the gat: Remote gateway over SSH (tunnels the gateway port to localhost). Format: user@host or user@host:port.
configHelpTranslations.Optional SSH identity file path (passed: Optional SSH identity file path (passed to ssh -i).
configHelpTranslations.CLI presentation controls for local comm: CLI presentation controls for local command output behavior such as banner and tagline style. Use this section to keep startup output aligned with operator preference without changing runtime behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Update-channel and startup-check behavio: Update-channel and startup-check behavior for keeping OpenClaw runtime versions current. Use conservative channels in production and more experimental channels only in controlled environments.
configHelpTranslations.Gateway runtime surface for bind mode, a: Gateway runtime surface for bind mode, auth, control UI, remote transport, and operational safety controls. Keep conservative defaults unless you intentionally expose the gateway beyond trusted local interfaces.
configHelpTranslations.Talk-mode voice synthesis settings for v: Talk-mode voice synthesis settings for voice identity, model selection, output format, and interruption behavior. Use this section to tune human-facing voice UX while controlling latency and cost.
configHelpTranslations.Provider-specific Talk settings keyed by: Provider-specific Talk settings keyed by provider id. During migration, prefer this over legacy talk.* keys.
Provider default voice ID for Talk mode.
configHelpTranslations.Optional provider voice alias map for Ta: Optional provider voice alias map for Talk directives.
Provider default model ID for Talk mode.
configHelpTranslations.Provider default output format for Talk: Provider default output format for Talk mode.
Provider API key for Talk mode.
configHelpTranslations.Legacy ElevenLabs default voice ID for T: Legacy ElevenLabs default voice ID for Talk mode. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.voiceId.
configHelpTranslations.Use this legacy ElevenLabs voice alias m: Use this legacy ElevenLabs voice alias map (for example {"Clawd":"EXAVITQu4vr4xnSDxMaL"}) only during migration. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.voiceAliases.
configHelpTranslations.Legacy ElevenLabs model ID for Talk mode: Legacy ElevenLabs model ID for Talk mode (default: eleven_v3). Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.modelId.
configHelpTranslations.Use this legacy ElevenLabs output format: Use this legacy ElevenLabs output format for Talk mode (for example pcm_44100 or mp3_44100_128) only during migration. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.outputFormat.
configHelpTranslations.Use this legacy ElevenLabs API key for T: Use this legacy ElevenLabs API key for Talk mode only during migration, and keep secrets in env-backed storage. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey (fallback: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY).
configHelpTranslations.If true (default), stop assistant speech: If true (default), stop assistant speech when the user starts speaking in Talk mode. Keep enabled for conversational turn-taking.
configHelpTranslations.ACP runtime controls for enabling dispat: ACP runtime controls for enabling dispatch, selecting backends, constraining allowed agent targets, and tuning streamed turn projection behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Global ACP feature gate. Keep disabled u: Global ACP feature gate. Keep disabled unless ACP runtime + policy are configured.
configHelpTranslations.Independent dispatch gate for ACP sessio: Independent dispatch gate for ACP session turns (default: true). Set false to keep ACP commands available while blocking ACP turn execution.
configHelpTranslations.Default ACP runtime backend id (for exam: Default ACP runtime backend id (for example: acpx). Must match a registered ACP runtime plugin backend.
configHelpTranslations.Fallback ACP target agent id used when A: Fallback ACP target agent id used when ACP spawns do not specify an explicit target.
configHelpTranslations.Allowlist of ACP target agent ids permit: Allowlist of ACP target agent ids permitted for ACP runtime sessions. Empty means no additional allowlist restriction.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum concurrently active ACP sessions: Maximum concurrently active ACP sessions across this gateway process.
configHelpTranslations.ACP streaming projection controls for ch: ACP streaming projection controls for chunk sizing, metadata visibility, and deduped delivery behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Coalescer idle flush window in milliseco: Coalescer idle flush window in milliseconds for ACP streamed text before block replies are emitted.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum chunk size for ACP streamed bloc: Maximum chunk size for ACP streamed block projection before splitting into multiple block replies.
configHelpTranslations.When true (default), suppress repeated A: When true (default), suppress repeated ACP status/tool projection lines in a turn while keeping raw ACP events unchanged.
configHelpTranslations.ACP delivery style: live streams project: ACP delivery style: live streams projected output incrementally, final_only buffers all projected ACP output until terminal turn events.
configHelpTranslations.Separator inserted before next visible a: Separator inserted before next visible assistant text when hidden ACP tool lifecycle events occurred (none|space|newline|paragraph). Default: paragraph.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum assistant output characters proj: Maximum assistant output characters projected per ACP turn before truncation notice is emitted.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum characters for projected ACP ses: Maximum characters for projected ACP session/update lines (tool/status updates).
configHelpTranslations.Per-sessionUpdate visibility overrides f: Per-sessionUpdate visibility overrides for ACP projection (for example usage_update, available_commands_update).
configHelpTranslations.Idle runtime TTL in minutes for ACP sess: Idle runtime TTL in minutes for ACP session workers before eligible cleanup.
configHelpTranslations.Optional operator install/setup command: Optional operator install/setup command shown by `/acp install` and `/acp doctor` when ACP backend wiring is missing.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in milliseconds for connecting t: Timeout in milliseconds for connecting to a remote CDP endpoint before failing the browser attach attempt. Increase for high-latency tunnels, or lower for faster failure detection.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in milliseconds for post-connect: Timeout in milliseconds for post-connect CDP handshake readiness checks against remote browser targets. Raise this for slow-start remote browsers and lower to fail fast in automation loops.
configHelpTranslations.Optional unicast DNS-SD domain for wide-: Optional unicast DNS-SD domain for wide-area discovery, such as openclaw.internal. Use this when you intentionally publish gateway discovery beyond local mDNS scopes.
configHelpTranslations.mDNS discovery configuration group for l: mDNS discovery configuration group for local network advertisement and discovery behavior tuning. Keep minimal mode for routine LAN discovery unless extra metadata is required.
configHelpTranslations.Approval strategy for when exec commands: Approval strategy for when exec commands require human confirmation before running. Use stricter ask behavior in shared channels and lower-friction settings in private operator contexts.
configHelpTranslations.Allowlist of target agent IDs permitted: Allowlist of target agent IDs permitted for agent_to_agent calls when orchestration is enabled. Use explicit allowlists to avoid uncontrolled cross-agent call graphs.
configHelpTranslations.Tool policy wrapper for spawned subagent: Tool policy wrapper for spawned subagents to restrict or expand tool availability compared to parent defaults. Use this to keep delegated agent capabilities scoped to task intent.
configHelpTranslations.Tool policy wrapper for sandboxed agent: Tool policy wrapper for sandboxed agent executions so sandbox runs can have distinct capability boundaries. Use this to enforce stronger safety in sandbox contexts.
configHelpTranslations.Web channel runtime settings for heartbe: Web channel runtime settings for heartbeat and reconnect behavior when operating web-based chat surfaces. Use reconnect values tuned to your network reliability profile and expected uptime needs.
configHelpTranslations.Enables the web channel runtime and rela: Enables the web channel runtime and related websocket lifecycle behavior. Keep disabled when web chat is unused to reduce active connection management overhead.
configHelpTranslations.Heartbeat interval in seconds for web ch: Heartbeat interval in seconds for web channel connectivity and liveness maintenance. Use shorter intervals for faster detection, or longer intervals to reduce keepalive chatter.
configHelpTranslations.Reconnect backoff policy for web channel: Reconnect backoff policy for web channel reconnect attempts after transport failure. Keep bounded retries and jitter tuned to avoid thundering-herd reconnect behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Initial reconnect delay in milliseconds: Initial reconnect delay in milliseconds before the first retry after disconnection. Use modest delays to recover quickly without immediate retry storms.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum reconnect backoff cap in millise: Maximum reconnect backoff cap in milliseconds to bound retry delay growth over repeated failures. Use a reasonable cap so recovery remains timely after prolonged outages.
configHelpTranslations.Exponential backoff multiplier used betw: Exponential backoff multiplier used between reconnect attempts in web channel retry loops. Keep factor above 1 and tune with jitter for stable large-fleet reconnect behavior.
configHelpTranslations.Randomization factor (0-1) applied to re: Randomization factor (0-1) applied to reconnect delays to desynchronize clients after outage events. Keep non-zero jitter in multi-client deployments to reduce synchronized spikes.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum reconnect attempts before giving: Maximum reconnect attempts before giving up for the current failure sequence (0 means no retries). Use finite caps for controlled failure handling in automation-sensitive environments.
configHelpTranslations.Optional URL prefix where the Control UI: Optional URL prefix where the Control UI is served (e.g. /openclaw).
configHelpTranslations.Optional filesystem root for Control UI: Optional filesystem root for Control UI assets (defaults to dist/control-ui).
configHelpTranslations.Allowed browser origins for Control UI/W: Allowed browser origins for Control UI/WebChat websocket connections (full origins only, e.g. https://control.example.com). Required for non-loopback Control UI deployments unless dangerous Host-header fallback is explicitly enabled.
configHelpTranslations.Push-delivery settings used by the gatew: Push-delivery settings used by the gateway when it needs to wake or notify paired devices. Configure relay-backed APNs here for official iOS builds; direct APNs auth remains env-based for local/manual builds.
configHelpTranslations.APNs delivery settings for iOS devices p: APNs delivery settings for iOS devices paired to this gateway. Use relay settings for official/TestFlight builds that register through the external push relay.
configHelpTranslations.External relay settings for relay-backed: External relay settings for relay-backed APNs sends. The gateway uses this relay for push.test, wake nudges, and reconnect wakes after a paired official iOS build publishes a relay-backed registration.
configHelpTranslations.Base HTTPS URL for the external APNs rel: Base HTTPS URL for the external APNs relay service used by official/TestFlight iOS builds. Keep this aligned with the relay URL baked into the iOS build so registration and send traffic hit the same deployment.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in milliseconds for relay send r: Timeout in milliseconds for relay send requests from the gateway to the APNs relay (default: 10000). Increase for slower relays or networks, or lower to fail wake attempts faster.
configHelpTranslations.Top-level media behavior shared across p: Top-level media behavior shared across providers and tools that handle inbound files. Keep defaults unless you need stable filenames for external processing pipelines or longer-lived inbound media retention.
configHelpTranslations.When enabled, uploaded media keeps its o: When enabled, uploaded media keeps its original filename instead of a generated temp-safe name. Turn this on when downstream automations depend on stable names, and leave off to reduce accidental filename leakage.
configHelpTranslations.Optional retention window in hours for p: Optional retention window in hours for persisted inbound media cleanup across the full media tree. Leave unset to preserve legacy behavior, or set values like 24 (1 day) or 168 (7 days) when you want automatic cleanup.
configHelpTranslations.Top-level binding rules for routing and: Top-level binding rules for routing and persistent ACP conversation ownership. Use type=route for normal routing and type=acp for persistent ACP harness bindings.
configHelpTranslations.Binding kind. Use \"route\" (or omit for: Binding kind. Use "route" (or omit for legacy route entries) for normal routing, and "acp" for persistent ACP conversation bindings.
configHelpTranslations.Target agent ID that receives traffic wh: Target agent ID that receives traffic when the corresponding binding match rule is satisfied. Use valid configured agent IDs only so routing does not fail at runtime.
configHelpTranslations.Match rule object for deciding when a bi: Match rule object for deciding when a binding applies, including channel and optional account/peer constraints. Keep rules narrow to avoid accidental agent takeover across contexts.
configHelpTranslations.Channel/provider identifier this binding: Channel/provider identifier this binding applies to, such as `telegram`, `discord`, or a plugin channel ID. Use the configured channel key exactly so binding evaluation works reliably.
configHelpTranslations.Optional account selector for multi-acco: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups so the binding applies only to one identity. Use this when account scoping is required for the route and leave unset otherwise.
configHelpTranslations.Optional peer matcher for specific conve: Optional peer matcher for specific conversations including peer kind and peer id. Use this when only one direct/group/channel target should be pinned to an agent.
configHelpTranslations.Peer conversation type: \"direct\", \"gr: Peer conversation type: "direct", "group", "channel", or legacy "dm" (deprecated alias for direct). Prefer "direct" for new configs and keep kind aligned with channel semantics.
configHelpTranslations.Conversation identifier used with peer m: Conversation identifier used with peer matching, such as a chat ID, channel ID, or group ID from the provider. Keep this exact to avoid silent non-matches.
configHelpTranslations.Optional Discord-style guild/server ID c: Optional Discord-style guild/server ID constraint for binding evaluation in multi-server deployments. Use this when the same peer identifiers can appear across different guilds.
configHelpTranslations.Optional team/workspace ID constraint us: Optional team/workspace ID constraint used by providers that scope chats under teams. Add this when you need bindings isolated to one workspace context.
configHelpTranslations.Optional role-based filter list used by: Optional role-based filter list used by providers that attach roles to chat context. Use this to route privileged or operational role traffic to specialized agents.
configHelpTranslations.Optional per-binding ACP overrides for b: Optional per-binding ACP overrides for bindings[].type=acp. This layer overrides agents.list[].runtime.acp defaults for the matched conversation.
configHelpTranslations.ACP session mode override for this bindi: ACP session mode override for this binding (persistent or oneshot).
configHelpTranslations.Human-friendly label for ACP status/diag: Human-friendly label for ACP status/diagnostics in this bound conversation.
configHelpTranslations.Working directory override for ACP sessi: Working directory override for ACP sessions created from this binding.
configHelpTranslations.ACP backend override for this binding (f: ACP backend override for this binding (falls back to agent runtime ACP backend, then global acp.backend).
configHelpTranslations.Broadcast routing map for sending the sa: Broadcast routing map for sending the same outbound message to multiple peer IDs per source conversation. Keep this minimal and audited because one source can fan out to many destinations.
configHelpTranslations.Delivery order for broadcast fan-out: \": Delivery order for broadcast fan-out: "parallel" sends to all targets concurrently, while "sequential" sends one-by-one. Use "parallel" for speed and "sequential" for stricter ordering/backpressure control.
configHelpTranslations.Per-source broadcast destination list wh: Per-source broadcast destination list where each key is a source peer ID and the value is an array of destination peer IDs. Keep lists intentional to avoid accidental message amplification.
configHelpTranslations.Enable targeted diagnostics logs by flag: Enable targeted diagnostics logs by flag (e.g. ["telegram.http"]). Supports wildcards like "telegram.*" or "*".
configHelpTranslations.Master toggle for diagnostics instrument: Master toggle for diagnostics instrumentation output in logs and telemetry wiring paths. Keep enabled for normal observability, and disable only in tightly constrained environments.
configHelpTranslations.Age threshold in milliseconds for emitti: Age threshold in milliseconds for emitting stuck-session warnings while a session remains in processing state. Increase for long multi-tool turns to reduce false positives; decrease for faster hang detection.
configHelpTranslations.Enables OpenTelemetry export pipeline fo: Enables OpenTelemetry export pipeline for traces, metrics, and logs based on configured endpoint/protocol settings. Keep disabled unless your collector endpoint and auth are fully configured.
configHelpTranslations.Collector endpoint URL used for OpenTele: Collector endpoint URL used for OpenTelemetry export transport, including scheme and port. Use a reachable, trusted collector endpoint and monitor ingestion errors after rollout.
configHelpTranslations.OTel transport protocol for telemetry ex: OTel transport protocol for telemetry export: "http/protobuf" or "grpc" depending on collector support. Use the protocol your observability backend expects to avoid dropped telemetry payloads.
configHelpTranslations.Additional HTTP/gRPC metadata headers se: Additional HTTP/gRPC metadata headers sent with OpenTelemetry export requests, often used for tenant auth or routing. Keep secrets in env-backed values and avoid unnecessary header sprawl.
configHelpTranslations.Service name reported in telemetry resou: Service name reported in telemetry resource attributes to identify this gateway instance in observability backends. Use stable names so dashboards and alerts remain consistent over deployments.
configHelpTranslations.Enable trace signal export to the config: Enable trace signal export to the configured OpenTelemetry collector endpoint. Keep enabled when latency/debug tracing is needed, and disable if you only want metrics/logs.
configHelpTranslations.Enable metrics signal export to the conf: Enable metrics signal export to the configured OpenTelemetry collector endpoint. Keep enabled for runtime health dashboards, and disable only if metric volume must be minimized.
configHelpTranslations.Enable log signal export through OpenTel: Enable log signal export through OpenTelemetry in addition to local logging sinks. Use this when centralized log correlation is required across services and agents.
configHelpTranslations.Trace sampling rate (0-1) controlling ho: Trace sampling rate (0-1) controlling how much trace traffic is exported to observability backends. Lower rates reduce overhead/cost, while higher rates improve debugging fidelity.
configHelpTranslations.Interval in milliseconds for periodic te: Interval in milliseconds for periodic telemetry flush from buffers to the collector. Increase to reduce export chatter, or lower for faster visibility during active incident response.
configHelpTranslations.Log cache trace snapshots for embedded a: Log cache trace snapshots for embedded agent runs (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.JSONL output path for cache trace logs (: JSONL output path for cache trace logs (default: $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/logs/cache-trace.jsonl).
configHelpTranslations.Include full message payloads in trace o: Include full message payloads in trace output (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Include prompt text in trace output (def: Include prompt text in trace output (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Include system prompt in trace output (d: Include system prompt in trace output (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Tool history window size for loop detect: Tool history window size for loop detection (default: 30).
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in seconds for each image unders: Timeout in seconds for each image understanding request before it is aborted. Increase for high-resolution analysis and lower it for latency-sensitive operator workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Controls post-compaction session memory: Controls post-compaction session memory reindex mode: "off", "async", or "await" (default: "async"). Use "await" for strongest freshness, "async" for lower compaction latency, and "off" only when session-memory sync is handled elsewhere.
configHelpTranslations.Adds extra directories or .md files to t: Adds extra directories or .md files to the memory index beyond default memory files. Use this when key reference docs live elsewhere in your repo; when multimodal memory is enabled, matching image/audio files under these paths are also eligible for indexing.
configHelpTranslations.Optional multimodal memory settings for: Optional multimodal memory settings for indexing image and audio files from configured extra paths. Keep this off unless your embedding model explicitly supports cross-modal embeddings, and set `memorySearch.fallback` to "none" while it is enabled. Matching files are uploaded to the configured remote embedding provider during indexing.
configHelpTranslations.Enables image/audio memory indexing from: Enables image/audio memory indexing from extraPaths. This currently requires Gemini embedding-2, keeps the default memory roots Markdown-only, disables memory-search fallback providers, and uploads matching binary content to the configured remote embedding provider.
configHelpTranslations.Sets the maximum bytes allowed per multi: Sets the maximum bytes allowed per multimodal file before it is skipped during memory indexing. Use this to cap upload cost and indexing latency, or raise it for short high-quality audio clips.
configHelpTranslations.Selects which multimodal file types are: Selects which multimodal file types are indexed from extraPaths: "image", "audio", or "all". Keep this narrow to avoid indexing large binary corpora unintentionally.
configHelpTranslations.Gemini embedding-2 only: chooses the out: Gemini embedding-2 only: chooses the output vector size for memory embeddings. Use 768, 1536, or 3072 (default), and expect a full reindex when you change it because stored vector dimensions must stay consistent.
configHelpTranslations.Forces a session memory-search reindex a: Forces a session memory-search reindex after compaction-triggered transcript updates (default: true). Keep enabled when compacted summaries must be immediately searchable, or disable to reduce write-time indexing pressure.
configHelpTranslations.Agent avatar (workspace-relative path, h: Agent avatar (workspace-relative path, http(s) URL, or data URI).
configHelpTranslations.Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius and avoid notifying channels for unrelated agents.
configHelpTranslations.Optional session-key filters matched as: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded to shared destinations.
