configHelpTranslations.Optional account selector for multi-acco: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when approvals must route through a specific account context. Use this only when the target channel has multiple configured identities.
configHelpTranslations.Channel/provider ID used for forwarded a: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id. Use valid channel IDs only so approvals do not silently fail due to unknown routes.
configHelpTranslations.Optional thread/topic target for channel: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded approvals. Use this to keep approval traffic contained in operational threads instead of main channels.
configHelpTranslations.Destination identifier inside the target: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider). Verify semantics per provider because destination format differs across channel integrations.
configHelpTranslations.Cooldown/backoff controls for temporary: Cooldown/backoff controls for temporary profile suppression after billing-related failures and retry windows. Use these to prevent rapid re-selection of profiles that are still blocked.
configHelpTranslations.Base backoff (hours) when a profile fail: Base backoff (hours) when a profile fails due to billing/insufficient credits (default: 5).
configHelpTranslations.Optional per-provider overrides for bill: Optional per-provider overrides for billing backoff (hours).
configHelpTranslations.Ordered auth profile IDs per provider (u: Ordered auth profile IDs per provider (used for automatic failover).
configHelpTranslations.Named auth profiles (provider + mode + o: Named auth profiles (provider + mode + optional email).
configHelpTranslations.Slack app-level token used for Socket Mo: Slack app-level token used for Socket Mode connections and event transport when enabled. Use least-privilege app scopes and store this token as a secret.
configHelpTranslations.Telegram-native exec approval routing an: Telegram-native exec approval routing and approver authorization. Enable this only when Telegram should act as an explicit exec-approval client for the selected bot account.
configHelpTranslations.Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for Telegram exec approvals, for example `["main", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to keep approval prompts scoped to the agents you actually operate from Telegram.
configHelpTranslations.Telegram user IDs allowed to approve exe: Telegram user IDs allowed to approve exec requests for this bot account. Use numeric Telegram user IDs; prompts are only delivered to these approvers when target includes dm.
configHelpTranslations.Enable Telegram exec approvals for this: Enable Telegram exec approvals for this account. When false or unset, Telegram messages/buttons cannot approve exec requests.
configHelpTranslations.Optional session-key filters matched as: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns before Telegram approval routing is used. Use narrow patterns so Telegram approvals only appear for intended sessions.
configHelpTranslations.Controls where Telegram approval prompts: Controls where Telegram approval prompts are sent: "dm" sends to approver DMs (default), "channel" sends to the originating Telegram chat/topic, and "both" sends to both. Channel delivery exposes the command text to the chat, so only use it in trusted groups/topics.
configHelpTranslations.Defines elevated command allow rules by: Defines elevated command allow rules by channel and sender for owner-level command surfaces. Use narrow provider-specific identities so privileged commands are not exposed to broad chat audiences.
configHelpTranslations.Allow bash chat command (`!`; `/bash` al: Allow bash chat command (`!`; `/bash` alias) to run host shell commands (default: false; requires tools.elevated).
configHelpTranslations.How long bash waits before backgrounding: How long bash waits before backgrounding (default: 2000; 0 backgrounds immediately).
configHelpTranslations.Allow /config chat command to read/write: Allow /config chat command to read/write config on disk (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Allow /debug chat command for runtime-on: Allow /debug chat command for runtime-only overrides (default: false).
configHelpTranslations.Registers native slash/menu commands wit: Registers native slash/menu commands with channels that support command registration (Discord, Slack, Telegram). Keep enabled for discoverability unless you intentionally run text-only command workflows.
configHelpTranslations.Registers native skill commands so users: Registers native skill commands so users can invoke skills directly from provider command menus where supported. Keep aligned with your skill policy so exposed commands match what operators expect.
configHelpTranslations.Explicit owner allowlist for owner-only: Explicit owner allowlist for owner-only tools/commands. Use channel-native IDs (optionally prefixed like "whatsapp:+15551234567"). '*' is ignored.
configHelpTranslations.Controls how owner IDs are rendered in t: Controls how owner IDs are rendered in the system prompt. Allowed values: raw, hash. Default: raw.
configHelpTranslations.Optional secret used to HMAC hash owner: Optional secret used to HMAC hash owner IDs when ownerDisplay=hash. Prefer env substitution.
configHelpTranslations.Allow /restart and gateway restart tool: Allow /restart and gateway restart tool actions (default: true).
configHelpTranslations.Enables text-command parsing in chat inp: Enables text-command parsing in chat input in addition to native command surfaces where available. Keep this enabled for compatibility across channels that do not support native command registration.
configHelpTranslations.Enables cron job execution for stored sc: Enables cron job execution for stored schedules managed by the gateway. Keep enabled for normal reminder/automation flows, and disable only to pause all cron execution without deleting jobs.
configHelpTranslations.Limits how many cron jobs can execute at: Limits how many cron jobs can execute at the same time when multiple schedules fire together. Use lower values to protect CPU/memory under heavy automation load, or raise carefully for higher throughput.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides the default retry policy for o: Overrides the default retry policy for one-shot jobs when they fail with transient errors (rate limit, overloaded, network, server_error). Omit to use defaults: maxAttempts 3, backoffMs [30000, 60000, 300000], retry all transient types.
configHelpTranslations.Backoff delays in ms for each retry atte: Backoff delays in ms for each retry attempt (default: [30000, 60000, 300000]). Use shorter values for faster retries.
configHelpTranslations.Max retries for one-shot jobs on transie: Max retries for one-shot jobs on transient errors before permanent disable (default: 3).
configHelpTranslations.Error types to retry: rate_limit, overlo: Error types to retry: rate_limit, overloaded, network, timeout, server_error. Use to restrict which errors trigger retries; omit to retry all transient types.
configHelpTranslations.Pruning controls for per-job cron run hi: Pruning controls for per-job cron run history files under `cron/runs/<jobId>.jsonl`, including size and line retention.
configHelpTranslations.How many trailing run-log lines to retai: How many trailing run-log lines to retain when a file exceeds maxBytes (default `2000`). Increase for longer forensic history or lower for smaller disks.
configHelpTranslations.Maximum bytes per cron run-log file befo: Maximum bytes per cron run-log file before pruning rewrites to the last keepLines entries (for example `2mb`, default `2000000`).
configHelpTranslations.Controls how long completed cron run ses: Controls how long completed cron run sessions are kept before pruning (`24h`, `7d`, `1h30m`, or `false` to disable pruning; default: `24h`). Use shorter retention to reduce storage growth on high-frequency schedules.
configHelpTranslations.Path to the cron job store file used to: Path to the cron job store file used to persist scheduled jobs across restarts. Set an explicit path only when you need custom storage layout, backups, or mounted volumes.
configHelpTranslations.Deprecated legacy fallback webhook URL u: Deprecated legacy fallback webhook URL used only for old jobs with `notify=true`. Migrate to per-job delivery using `delivery.mode="webhook"` plus `delivery.to`, and avoid relying on this global field.
configHelpTranslations.Bearer token attached to cron webhook PO: Bearer token attached to cron webhook POST deliveries when webhook mode is used. Prefer secret/env substitution and rotate this token regularly if shared webhook endpoints are internet-reachable.
configHelpTranslations.Allowlist of agent IDs that hook mapping: Allowlist of agent IDs that hook mappings are allowed to target when selecting execution agents. Use this to constrain automation events to dedicated service agents.
configHelpTranslations.Allowlist of accepted session-key prefix: Allowlist of accepted session-key prefixes for inbound hook requests when caller-provided keys are enabled. Use narrow prefixes to prevent arbitrary session-key injection.
configHelpTranslations.Target agent ID for mapping execution wh: Target agent ID for mapping execution when action routing should not use defaults. Use dedicated automation agents to isolate webhook behavior from interactive operator sessions.
configHelpTranslations.Caps how much QMD text can be injected i: Caps how much QMD text can be injected into one turn across all hits. Use lower values to control prompt bloat and latency; raise only when context is consistently truncated.
configHelpTranslations.Limits how many QMD hits are returned in: Limits how many QMD hits are returned into the agent loop for each recall request (default: 6). Increase for broader recall context, or lower to keep prompts tighter and faster.
configHelpTranslations.Caps per-result snippet length extracted: Caps per-result snippet length extracted from QMD hits in characters (default: 700). Lower this when prompts bloat quickly, and raise only if answers consistently miss key details.
configHelpTranslations.Sets per-query QMD search timeout in mil: Sets per-query QMD search timeout in milliseconds (default: 4000). Increase for larger indexes or slower environments, and lower to keep request latency bounded.
configHelpTranslations.Defines which sessions/channels are elig: Defines which sessions/channels are eligible for QMD recall using session.sendPolicy-style rules. Keep default direct-only scope unless you intentionally want cross-chat memory sharing.
configHelpTranslations.Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd embed: Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd embed` cycle in milliseconds (default: 120000). Increase for heavier embedding workloads or slower hardware, and lower to fail fast under tight SLAs.
configHelpTranslations.Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd updat: Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd update` cycle in milliseconds (default: 120000). Raise this for larger collections; lower it when you want quicker failure detection in automation.
Override default status reaction emojis. Keys: thinking, compacting, tool, coding, web, done, error, stallSoft, stallHard. Must be valid Telegram reaction emojis.
configHelpTranslations.Automatic AWS Bedrock model discovery se: Automatic AWS Bedrock model discovery settings used to synthesize provider model entries from account visibility. Keep discovery scoped and refresh intervals conservative to reduce API churn.
configHelpTranslations.Fallback context-window value applied to: Fallback context-window value applied to discovered models when provider metadata lacks explicit limits. Use realistic defaults to avoid oversized prompts that exceed true provider constraints.
configHelpTranslations.Fallback max-token value applied to disc: Fallback max-token value applied to discovered models without explicit output token limits. Use conservative defaults to reduce truncation surprises and unexpected token spend.
configHelpTranslations.Refresh cadence for Bedrock discovery po: Refresh cadence for Bedrock discovery polling in seconds to detect newly available models over time. Use longer intervals in production to reduce API cost and control-plane noise.
configHelpTranslations.AWS region used for Bedrock discovery ca: AWS region used for Bedrock discovery calls when discovery is enabled for your deployment. Use the region where your Bedrock models are provisioned to avoid empty discovery results.
configHelpTranslations.Controls provider catalog behavior: \"me: Controls provider catalog behavior: "merge" keeps built-ins and overlays your custom providers, while "replace" uses only your configured providers. In "merge", matching provider IDs preserve non-empty agent models.json baseUrl values, while apiKey values are preserved only when the provider is not SecretRef-managed in current config/auth-profile context; SecretRef-managed providers refresh apiKey from current source markers, and matching model contextWindow/maxTokens use the higher value between explicit and implicit entries.
configHelpTranslations.Provider API adapter selection controlli: Provider API adapter selection controlling request/response compatibility handling for model calls. Use the adapter that matches your upstream provider protocol to avoid feature mismatch.
configHelpTranslations.Selects provider auth style: \"api-key\": Selects provider auth style: "api-key" for API key auth, "token" for bearer token auth, "oauth" for OAuth credentials, and "aws-sdk" for AWS credential resolution. Match this to your provider requirements.
configHelpTranslations.When true, credentials are sent via the: When true, credentials are sent via the HTTP Authorization header even if alternate auth is possible. Use this only when your provider or proxy explicitly requires Authorization forwarding.
configHelpTranslations.Static HTTP headers merged into provider: Static HTTP headers merged into provider requests for tenant routing, proxy auth, or custom gateway requirements. Use this sparingly and keep sensitive header values in secrets.
configHelpTranslations.Controls whether OpenClaw injects `optio: Controls whether OpenClaw injects `options.num_ctx` for Ollama providers configured with the OpenAI-compatible adapter (`openai-completions`). Default is true. Set false only if your proxy/upstream rejects unknown `options` payload fields.
configHelpTranslations.Declared model list for a provider inclu: Declared model list for a provider including identifiers, metadata, and optional compatibility/cost hints. Keep IDs exact to provider catalog values so selection and fallback resolve correctly.
configHelpTranslations.Approval routing controls for forwarding: Approval routing controls for forwarding exec approval requests to chat destinations outside the originating session. Keep this disabled unless operators need explicit out-of-band approval visibility.
configHelpTranslations.Model catalog root for provider definiti: Model catalog root for provider definitions, merge/replace behavior, and optional Bedrock discovery integration. Keep provider definitions explicit and validated before relying on production failover paths.
configHelpTranslations.Authentication profile root used for mul: Authentication profile root used for multi-profile provider credentials and cooldown-based failover ordering. Keep profiles minimal and explicit so automatic failover behavior stays auditable.
configHelpTranslations.Controls chat command surfaces, owner ga: Controls chat command surfaces, owner gating, and elevated command access behavior across providers. Keep defaults unless you need stricter operator controls or broader command availability.
configHelpTranslations.Global scheduler settings for stored cro: Global scheduler settings for stored cron jobs, run concurrency, delivery fallback, and run-session retention. Keep defaults unless you are scaling job volume or integrating external webhook receivers.
configHelpTranslations.Overrides the canonical main session key: Overrides the canonical main session key used for continuity when dmScope or routing logic points to "main". Use a stable value only if you intentionally need custom session anchoring.
configHelpTranslations.Enable filesystem watching for skill-def: Enable filesystem watching for skill-definition changes so updates can be applied without full process restart. Keep enabled in development workflows and disable in immutable production images.
configHelpTranslations.Debounce window in milliseconds for coal: Debounce window in milliseconds for coalescing rapid skill file changes before reload logic runs. Increase to reduce reload churn on frequent writes, or lower for faster edit feedback.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in seconds for each video unders: Timeout in seconds for each video understanding request before cancellation. Use conservative values in interactive channels and longer values for offline or batch-heavy processing.
configHelpTranslations.Cache TTL in minutes for web_fetch resul: Cache TTL in minutes for web_fetch results.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in seconds for Firecrawl request: Timeout in seconds for Firecrawl requests.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in seconds for web_fetch request: Timeout in seconds for web_fetch requests.
configHelpTranslations.Override User-Agent header for web_fetch: Override User-Agent header for web_fetch requests.
configHelpTranslations.Cache TTL in minutes for web_search resu: Cache TTL in minutes for web_search results.
configHelpTranslations.Timeout in seconds for web_search reques: Timeout in seconds for web_search requests.
